records.summary: The app-builder direction is an AI-first app-building ontology; the June 7 v1 recalibration packet currently leads implementation scope.
records.rationale: The June 2026 interviews and the 2026-06-03 cleanup supersede the older starter/golden frame as top-level authority, and the 2026-06-07 recalibration packet narrows the immediate v1 work back to truthful substrate gaps before broad composition climbing. The durable direction is app-building affordances, input dependencies, application design patterns, status/recommendation policy, component/control manifests, style inputs, SourcePlan effects, pressure fixtures, and semantic-runtime verification.
guidance: For app-builder v1 implementation, read `.temp/app-builder-v1-scope-recalibration-packet-2026-06-07.md` first until a later packet supersedes it. Use the June 1-3 synthesis/interview files as background texture and provenance when ambiguity remains, not as co-equal current scope.

The current direction is read-only breadth before more generation: framework capability terrain -> app-builder policy/status projection -> application design patterns -> app-building affordance/move graph -> input contracts and supplied-input provenance -> control/style/component manifest substrate -> executable lowerers for recommendable source-lowering-implemented rows -> SourcePlan effects -> semantic-runtime reopen/verification/findings.

The June 7 packet pulls immediate v1 attention to runnable app startup/root awareness, control affordance expansion, validation policy floor, central default labels/messages, LLM/seed/local data handoff, @aurelia/state posture, lean accessibility, and existing control library/plugin detection/reporting. Routed-detail `with.bind` taste has a first fix in the routed collection/detail lowerer: default generated detail source should keep explicit item expressions such as `taskItem.title` and `state.assigneeLabelForTaskItem(taskItem)`, leaving `with.bind` as an explicit structural part rather than a default compactness strategy. Treat `$parent.state.*($this)` in generated app-builder detail fixtures as a regression canary. Do not keep broad composition ascent ahead of remaining gaps just because local maintenance tasks are abundant.
When input is missing, return structured missing-input rows plus options. Do not silently choose a sample domain, identity field, plugin, visual style, state boundary, or routing policy.

Input contracts should declare accepted supplied-input sources. Keep caller/AI supplied domain intent, explicit public presets, request-local decision bundles, future policy, and deterministic existing-app facts distinct; existing-app facts may inform an AI, but they are not semantic-runtime business-domain inference.

Input contracts should link to fine-grained input facets through `has-input-facet` relation rows. Facets make missing input actionable for an AI caller, but they must not invent values or silently satisfy the parent contract.

Input facet statuses should use explicit authority helpers rather than a blanket TBD helper. Operator-confirmed facets come from interview decisions, source-backed facets come from SourcePlan/app-fact/framework/accessibility evidence, and only unresolved visual payload frontiers should remain TBD with notes.

Input dependency relation rows may carry row-local input facet selections when a broad contract would be too noisy. Omitted facet selection means the row intentionally depends on the whole contract; selected facets must flow through ontology-catalog, target-catalog, input-readiness, and detail queries consistently. Exact `targetRefs` are the lossless selector for known rows; compact `targetSelectors` are the AI-facing kind/id selector normalized by semantic-runtime into exact domain-bearing refs before target-catalog, input-readiness, or source-lowering preflight graph operations. Exact target drill-downs should preserve normalized caller order rather than descriptor-family order. Domain, kind, status, and surface filters are menu filters and continuations should not widen exact refs back to kind-only filters.

Use `input-contract-detail` after `input-readiness` when a caller needs payload/schema detail for missing facets. `suppliedInputs` can be contract-wide markers, facet-scoped markers, or facet payload carriers; input-readiness validates modeled facet payloads against input-contract-detail schemas and reports invalid payloads as typed issues.

Unfiltered `input-contract-detail` is compact by default: it returns contracts/facets, payload schema states, and schema counts, but omits modeled payload schema bodies unless the caller selects or filters contracts/facets/schema states or explicitly requests `includePayloadSchemas`. Keep this contract so broad MCP/menu calls do not dump every input payload shape before the AI has selected what it needs.

`input-contract-detail` reports `sourceLoweringConsumerCount` and selected/detail `sourceLoweringConsumerRows` for input facets through `input-source-lowering-consumers.ts`; public MCP callers request rows with `includeSourceLoweringConsumers`, and the MCP schema/contract should stay aligned with the semantic-runtime request type. Compact answers with consumer counts should continue back to `input-contract-detail` with consumer rows enabled; selected/detail answers with consumer rows should continue to consumer `target-catalog` rows and `source-lowering-preflight` so counts are actionable without pretending input facets are executable targets. Treat these as payload-consumer evidence, not as source-lowering implementation for the facet itself: `DomainActions` can feed source-lowerable native buttons, native submit forms, or domain-command methods while `DomainActions.sourceLoweringImplemented` remains false.

`input-contract-detail` also reports `sourceLoweringValueSupportCount` and selected/detail `sourceLoweringValueSupportRows` through `input-facet-value-support.ts` for enum-like values inside modeled input facets. Compact answers with support counts should continue back to `input-contract-detail` with `includeSourceLoweringValueSupport`; selected/detail answers may continue to value-support target refs when present. Treat these rows as value-level support evidence, not recommendation/defaulting or proof that every action/relationship value has source realization. Current `DomainActions` value support is native event binding, caller-owned TypeScript methods, and narrow derived local `create`/`complete` methods; current `DomainRelationships` value support is narrow `reference-one` source realization for router-backed list/detail relationships and local view-model collection reference lookups, local-only `reference-many` collection lookups through choice-set identity arrays, local-only `owns-one` owned child objects, local-only `owns-many` owned child arrays with joined label display, and local-only `nested-value-object` identityless value source with label display. Owned child/value-object editing, lifecycle/state-machine semantics, and service-backed relationship workflows remain deferred.

Public app-builder ontology detail queries are selector-sensitive: explicit row ids or row-family filters get rich joins by default, while unscoped detail calls return base rows and counts unless the caller opts into each nested join with `include*` flags. The intended path is catalog/readiness first, selected detail second; do not reintroduce unscoped detail calls that expand the whole ontology graph for MCP callers. Compact detail answers should still collect their base row ids into typed continuations to selected detail queries, so broad MCP calls remain small without becoming terminal pages.

`source-lowering-preflight` preserves associated effect contract ids and exposes source-lowering availability by surface kind. `canRequestSourceLowering` is an eligibility bit after durable input readiness, policy satisfaction, and target-specific facts; it does not mean every per-call request field has already been supplied. Compact preflight answers expose `requiredRequestFieldTargetCount` and `requiredSourceLoweringRequestFieldCount`, and each row exposes `sourceLoweringRequestFieldSummary`; callers must inspect those before treating a surface as fully callable. One-target native controls expose `TargetInvocation` for fragments and `SourcePlanPreview` for the file-wrapper surface. Fragment-composition targets should continue to `source-lowering-composition` only when the FragmentComposition surface has no remaining required request fields, so Native Submit Form and collection projections are not mistaken for single-control invocations and AppSection does not advertise composition lowering before `childCompositions` is supplied. SourcePlan-preview targets can continue to `source-lowering-source-plan` only after explicit source placement and other SourcePlan-preview required request fields are supplied; `SourceRoot` and `SourceTargetPath` are the read-only placement facets, while `rootDir`, HTML-wrapper `templatePath`, and component-pair `sourceTargetPath` request fields are resolved transport forms. SourcePlan preview may consume resolved transport fields where a surface exposes them or supplied `SourcePlacement` facet payloads, but conflicting values must block with typed issues. Missing placement must be reported, not invented. Target-specific preflight requirements, such as range numeric bounds or unsupported Collection Table query features, are the single gate for facts outside ordinary input readiness; invocation, composition, and direct SourcePlan answers should bridge those requirements rather than rechecking the same target policy locally. Direct SourcePlan targets should route through `directSourcePlanTargetFrame` for preflight rows, descriptor-backed admission issues, target refs, and effect contract ids before adding target-specific source facts. Blocked preflight rows should continue to exact input-contract detail for both coarse missing durable inputs and finer target-specific source facts: for example DomainModel/DomainFields when a native control lacks domain fields, DomainModel/DomainFields for range bounds, or CollectionProjection table/query facets for collection-table query blockers. Lowerers may still validate the exact selected field/action/value they are about to spend after the target-level gate has cleared. Invocation, composition, direct SourcePlan, and component-pair answers should carry continuations to the wrapper so callers do not infer nested request envelopes manually.

Preflight is now split into selection, readiness, rows, and summary frames. Future source-lowering gates should reuse `sourceLoweringPreflightSelectionFrame`, `sourceLoweringPreflightReadinessFrame`, `sourceLoweringPreflightRowsFrame`, and `sourceLoweringPreflightSummaryFrame` instead of threading target normalization, input readiness, policy satisfaction, request-field summaries, or target-specific requirements through lowerers.

Source-lowering lowerers should request preflight input dependencies only for detail-mode answers. Default invocation, composition, and direct SourcePlan answers still run preflight and keep compact readiness summaries, target requirement issues, and policy-satisfaction facts, but `preflightRow.inputDependencies` belongs behind `includePreflight: true` so nested field/control lowerers do not repeat full dependency rows across every selected field.

`source-lowering-source-plan` now emits `sourcePlanWitnessRows` derived from the returned SourcePlan. Treat them as the concrete SourcePlanPreview witness product for pre-write policy, file, contribution, dependency, script, and project-tooling inspection. They do not duplicate generated source text, prove semantic-runtime reopen, or change effect-contract/sourceLoweringImplemented status.

`source-lowering-source-plan` keeps SourcePlan witness detail opt-in for public answers. `sourcePlanWitnessCount` is visible by default; request `includeSourcePlanWitnessRows` only for fixture/idempotency review, deep SourcePlan provenance inspection, or other detail-mode callers that truly need row payloads.

`source-lowering-source-plan` keeps per-file SourcePlan contribution ledgers opt-in for public answers. Compact source plans carry generated file text, project tooling, `contributionCount`, and witness counts by default; request `includeSourcePlanContributions` only for provenance/debug callers that need `SourcePlan.files[].contributions` rather than compact witness rows.

`source-lowering-source-plan` keeps selected source-lowering result objects opt-in for public answers. `sourceLoweringSelectionKind` and `sourceLoweringResultDetailsIncluded` are visible by default; request `includeSourceLoweringResultDetails` only when inspecting the nested selected invocation/composition/component-pair/app-shell/DI-state/local-state result tree. Compact SourcePlan answers should carry final source text, expected-effect counts/kinds, request-field summaries, witness counts, control-use counts, and selected-kind metadata without repeating the full derivation tree.

`source-lowering-source-plan` keeps generated control-use inventory rows opt-in for public answers. `controlUseInventoryRowCount` is visible by default; request `includeControlUseInventoryRows` only for fixture/idempotency review, MCP/IDE control inventory inspection, or other detail-mode callers that need the generated control/action/value-channel row payload.

`source-lowering-source-plan` emits `sourceLoweringRequestFieldSummary` by default and full `sourceLoweringRequestFields` only when `includeSourceLoweringRequestFields` is true. Target-owned request fields still belong to target catalog/preflight; assembly-owned fields such as component-pair `sourceTargetPath`, `templatePath`, and `sourceLoweringComponentPair` belong on the SourcePlan answer because no single ontology target owns that envelope.

`source-lowering-source-plan` also emits compact decision-bundle accounting for the whole selected request tree. Counts are visible by default; expansion rows are opt-in through `includeDecisionBundleExpansionRows`. Keep placement resolution narrower than accounting: root/template/source-target path selection may spend only placement-relevant explicit inputs and decision bundles, while accounting can include nested invocation, composition, and component-pair request envelopes for public review.

Decision-bundle target refs are preserved onto supplied-input expansion rows. Readiness, preflight, one-target invocation, composition, and direct SourcePlan preview should use `appBuilderSuppliedInputsForTarget` or `appBuilderSuppliedInputsWithDecisionBundlesForTarget` whenever a target ref is available, so explicit choices for one co-present target do not satisfy neighboring targets by accident.

Nested composition requests should inherit the parent's full supplied-input context first and let the nested target apply target filtering for itself. Do not filter parent inputs through a wrapper target before lowering `childCompositions` or `fulfilledContentComposition`; target-scoped decisions for nested tables/forms/status regions must survive wrapper composition boundaries.

`source-lowering-source-plan` also emits `expectedEffectCount` and `expectedEffectKinds` by default for direct full-app SourcePlan targets. Full `expectedEffects` rows are opt-in through `includeExpectedEffectRows`; they are projected from fixture-verification `ExpectedSemanticEffect` records through `semantic-effect-witness.ts` and are post-reopen semantic promises, not pre-write SourcePlan witnesses or proof that the generated source has already been verified.

Source-lowering pressure fixture manifests should consume `AppBuilderSourceLoweringGalleryPlan.expectedEffects`, not a materializer-local fixture-id expected-effect switch. Direct full-app gallery plans should reuse the same expected-effect helpers as public SourcePlan previews so pressure fixtures and MCP/IDE preview rows describe one semantic promise.

`source-lowering-request-field.ts` is the enum-backed surface-scoped per-call request contract for spending selected source-lowering targets after durable input readiness passes. Target catalog, preflight, and SourcePlan answers should expose `sourceLoweringRequestFieldSummary` by default and full `sourceLoweringRequestFields` only when `includeSourceLoweringRequestFields` is true, so compact MCP/menu calls keep counts while detail callers can inspect fields such as `fieldName`, `bindingExpression`, `valueDomainExpression`, `buttonText`, `messageKind`, `actionName`, `methodParameters`, `methodBodyStatements`, `promiseExpression`, `collectionExpression`, `itemLocalName`, `fieldNames`, `submitButtonText`, `sortHandlerExpressions`, `rootDir`, `templatePath`, `sourceTargetPath`, and nested invocation/composition/component-pair/app-shell/DI-state envelopes before invoking the selected surface; these are request contract rows outside durable input facets, and app-builder must not invent them. Target catalog and preflight compact summaries should omit surface-scoped request property name arrays until request-field detail is requested. The summary is the compact count rollup over the same rows for target-catalog, preflight, and SourcePlan answers; it is not a separate defaulting authority, readiness authority, or successful-answer missing-field state. Public source-lowering continuations should use the summary as a surface-scoped request-contract gate and only emit invocation/composition/SourcePlan follow-ups when the target continuation can be formed without required per-call request fields. Request-field usage extraction and registry coverage now live in sibling files behind the request-field barrel: fixture materializers and future coverage tools should use the shared usage/coverage helpers instead of carrying script-local request-tree walkers. Coverage summaries may be scoped by registry owner kind and surface kind for lane-specific review; focused detail rows should match that same scoped row set instead of preserving unrelated wrapper fields.

SourcePlan wrapper continuations from source-lowering invocation or composition answers must be fragment-backed. If a selected invocation/composition returns no fragments because target selection, field/action selection, or another request-specific fact is missing or invalid, the answer should expose issues and detail/verification continuations rather than advertising an empty SourcePlan preview.

`source-lowering-surface.ts` is the exact-target registry for callable app-builder source-lowering surfaces. Keep it synchronized with public `sourceLoweringImplemented` status through the app-builder query-surface contract instead of adding projection-local target sets.

`app-shell` is now a direct `SourcePlanPreview` ontology target through `sourceLoweringAppShell`, not an invocation/composition target. It emits the minimal non-routed root shell from explicit SourceRoot, SourceNaming.appName, SourceFileLayout.resourceCarrier, and AureliaConventionPolicy input; baseline project tooling is a SourcePlan consequence, and routed shells must remain a separate future ontology target instead of being hidden inside AppShell.

`di-state-class` is now a direct `SourcePlanPreview` ontology target through `sourceLoweringDiStateClass`. It spends caller domain entity/fields, SourceRoot, SourceTargetPath, and optional seed records to emit one explicit TypeScript state model file. It deliberately does not emit command methods, router wiring, or state-plugin store code; those stay separate source-lowering mechanics.

`local-view-model-state` is now a direct `SourcePlanPreview` ontology target through `sourceLoweringLocalViewModelState`. It spends caller domain fields, SourceRoot, SourceTargetPath, explicit local-state policy, and SourceNaming.baseName to emit one component view-model TypeScript file with either explicit scalar field defaults or explicit caller-supplied local collection state. Missing local-state policy must block source output rather than silently selecting scalar view-model state. It deliberately does not emit command/action methods, service calls, router wiring, or state-plugin store code; those stay separate source-lowering mechanics.

`appBuilderLocalViewModelStateSourceFragments` is the reusable local-state fragment emitter. Use it when a component pair or future composition needs local view-model state as TypeScript top-level declarations plus class members instead of a standalone file. Named domain value sets should resolve through `appBuilderDomainFieldResolvedOptions` so finite option-domain type aliases and option members stay consistent across direct file, component-pair, and control-lowering surfaces. Caller-supplied collection state should spend the `view-model-local-collection` policy so collection/table fixtures do not grow unused scalar field state.

When generated local field state and generated local collection state coexist in one component, let the collection/entity fragment own shared finite-option type aliases and keep local field fragments to option members plus draft fields. This avoids duplicate type aliases while preserving standalone field-state and standalone collection-state source plans.

`sourceLoweringComponentPair` is a SourcePlan assembly surface, not a new ontology target kind. It combines one template-producing source-lowering composition with zero or more TypeScript class-member source-lowering invocations into a custom-element TypeScript/template SourcePlan pair. Use it when a selected pattern needs both authored template syntax and companion view-model methods; do not make `DomainCommandAction` look like a standalone SourcePlan target just because its class-member fragment can be assembled there.

`sourceLoweringComponentPair` may also include nested local view-model state fragments. Those fragments should carry direct `SourceLoweringTarget` origins while child command invocations keep invocation origins and template fragments keep composition/part origins, so SourcePlan previews can explain generated state, declarations, template, and methods without reparsing source.

Domain relationship/action input detail is structured caller terrain, not a source-lowering promise. Relationships describe references, ownership, and nested value objects; actions describe commands before event/service/state/route realization is chosen.

App-builder application patterns should only claim `AppBuilderApplicationDataShapeId.Relationship` when the selected pattern or source-lowering path consumes `DomainRelationships` or otherwise models a real caller/domain relationship. Flat entity, field, collection, list, card, table, router-backed list/detail, local view-model state, and DI-state lowerers should not carry the relationship data-shape label just because the relationship vocabulary exists.

Route-backed area and app-section affordances should not require `DomainRelationships` for ordinary entity/field route-backed list/detail generation. Reintroduce the relationship facet only when a future relationship-oriented app section, related-object control, owned-child section, or nested value-object lowerer can spend it honestly.

`DomainCommandAction` is source-lowering implemented as a caller-owned TypeScript class-member fragment and should not claim route-backed navigation, router realization, or route semantic effects. Route/navigation semantics belong to `RouteNavigationAction` and router-backed SourcePlan lowerers; do not hide router calls inside arbitrary command `methodBodyStatements`.

Generated fixture domain-model kind coverage is caller-input coverage, not semantic realization proof. Use the generated markdown summary for action kind, action-scope, and relationship-kind counts, but treat uncovered standalone navigation outside router-backed topology and relationship editing/lifecycle/service workflows as app-design/source-lowering frontier evidence until real lowerers can spend those semantics.

Domain value-set input detail is distinct from domain field detail. Choice-like controls should spend named finite option domains rather than hiding option requirements inside field rows, accessibility payloads, or collection projection.

Domain field input may include optional `defaultValue` using the same value shape as seed-record values. Local view-model state source lowering spends it for draft/scalar initialization so generated app fixtures can show explicit caller defaults rather than silently selecting the first finite option. Finite choice fields may also include optional `optionTypeName`; source lowerers should spend that caller/domain alias for one option value. Field-local finite option-domain source names should describe one selectable value, so choice-set fields singularize the final source-name word for option value aliases and option members (`MyAppLabel`, `labelOptions`) unless explicit `optionTypeName` or reusable `valueSetName` input says otherwise.

Source-placement input detail should spend source-plan substrate where possible. Source naming points at source-name derivation and SourcePatternParameterValue rows; source project tooling points at SourcePlanProjectTooling rather than app-builder-local package/tsconfig prose.

SeedRecordSet input detail is a modeled dynamic-record payload: keys are meaningful only against the selected domain descriptor, while values follow the AppBuilderSeedRecord primitive or primitive-array union accepted by current source lowerers. Do not replace selected-domain validation with a static seed-record schema. Seed records are public generated-app source when they are materialized, so generated fixtures should use ordinary domain-shaped sample data rather than review, fixture, or lowerer-behavior prose.

Use `affordance-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected app-building moves joined to input readiness, input contract detail, promised effect contracts, associated application patterns, and declared follow-up affordances. It reports factual graph structure and must not rank or invent next moves.

Use `application-pattern-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected application design patterns joined to input readiness, input contract detail, coordinated collection/control/style concept rows, and associated affordances. It is the design-mechanic drill-down, not source lowering.

Application-pattern companion edges expose commonly coordinated design mechanics, such as collection presentation plus loading/empty/error state, without making those companions hidden defaults or effect/source-lowering inheritance.

Application pattern rows carry state, navigation, data, interaction, and Aurelia-realization shape axes. These axes make pattern rows more algebraic for menus and future lowerers, but they are not recommendation policy and do not claim every coordinated shape is currently source-lowerable.

Application pattern rows also carry broad `semanticEffectKinds`, and `application-pattern-detail` joins those ids to fixture-verification semantic-effect descriptors. Treat those effect kinds and descriptors as verification terrain and migration pressure away from legacy composition-only `verificationEffectKinds`, not exact expected-effect counts, generation readiness, or recommendation policy.

Use `collection-concept-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected collection source/query/projection/table concepts joined to input readiness, input contract detail, coordinating application patterns, control/style concept rows, and associated affordances. It is the collection drill-down, not presentation choice, column invention, or source lowering.

Collection feature descriptors explain caller-selectable CollectionQueryFeatures payload values and map them to collection concepts plus identity pressures. Keep them as input-value descriptors unless a future need makes them true target-catalog ontology rows. Recommendation policy should keep collection facets narrow: list/card projection depends on display fields, table projection depends on table columns, and selected query features add constraints only when the caller asks for those rungs.

Collection identity policy is feature-driven. Object identity is valid for simple local repeats and close domain/view-model boundaries; scalar, composite, or caller-supplied stable keys become input when explicit keyed repeat, row selection, batch actions, edit buffers, cross-page state, or route boundaries require them.

Use `control-pattern-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected native-first or deferred rich control patterns joined to input readiness, input contract detail, concrete native leaf-control descriptors, coordinating application patterns, control-realization policy rows, manifest/style/visual policy rows, and associated affordances. It is the control drill-down, not visual design, widget-library selection, wrapper extraction, or source lowering.

Use `control-manifest-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected control/component manifest rows joined to manifest field descriptors, input readiness, input contract detail, coordinating application patterns, control pattern/descriptor rows, style/visual rows, and associated affordances. It is the manifest-contract drill-down, not external manifest projection, component API design, style choice, or source lowering.

ControlPattern rows should point at APP_BUILDER_CONTROLS descriptors when concrete native control semantics already exist. Those descriptors own value channels, transport kinds, slots, and built-in syntax refs; do not copy that substrate into ontology row prose or parallel tables.

App-builder source-lowering invocation derives native control-pattern to leaf-control mapping from `APP_BUILDER_CONTROL_PATTERN_ROWS.leafControlIds`. Keep that catalog as the source of truth instead of reintroducing local NativeTextInput/TextInput-style switches.

ControlPattern rows should stay split at value-contract granularity. Boolean checkbox, checkbox-list membership, single select, multi select, date input, and range input are separate rows even when native elements or binding syntax overlap; group them through application patterns, not hidden descriptor arrays.

Input-dependent ontology rows should declare row-local facet selections when a whole contract would ask for unrelated facts. Affordances, application patterns, collection concepts, policy axes, control patterns, control manifests, and visual policies should spend the shared relation/readiness/detail path; broad intake rows can intentionally keep whole contracts visible. Direct styling-mechanism rows are selectable mechanism values: the StylingMechanism policy axis owns mechanism-choice readiness, and visual policies that spend style material own visual payload readiness. `visual-input-missing` is the absence-reporting visual-policy posture and should have no `VisualStyleInput` dependency or payload detail rows. Recommendation-policy overrides should add context not already present in relation-backed readiness; do not duplicate a broad visual-input applicability row when the graph already names the exact VisualStyleInput facets.

Use `effect-contract-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected promised effects joined to concrete witness descriptors, public app-query rows for semantic-runtime query witnesses, manifest rows/field descriptors for component-control witnesses, and back to the affordances that promise them, plus those affordances' input readiness, input contract detail, and associated application patterns. Semantic-runtime reopen rows can also include fixture-verification semantic-effect descriptors so expected-effect promises have inspectable product/query-family terrain. It is the effect-to-affordance/witness drill-down, not verification execution, satisfaction proof, or source lowering.

Effect contract rows should name a witness boundary and witness families, and `APP_BUILDER_EFFECT_WITNESS_DESCRIPTOR_ROWS` should name enum-backed witness fields. Future SourcePlan, semantic-runtime reopen, control/component manifest, and existing-app fact work should spend those descriptors, `APP_BUILDER_CONTROL_MANIFEST_FIELD_DESCRIPTOR_ROWS`, and `EXPECTED_SEMANTIC_EFFECT_KIND_DESCRIPTOR_ROWS` instead of adding effect-specific prose or bespoke verifier flags.

`relation-index.ts` owns the full unfiltered app-builder ontology relation graph for internal cross-domain projections. `ontology-catalog` may return filtered relation rows for public answers, but input-readiness, effect-targeting, source-lowering surfaces, and detail joins should use `APP_BUILDER_ONTOLOGY_RELATION_ROWS`, `appBuilderOntologyRelationsFrom(...)`, or `appBuilderOntologyRelationsTo(...)` rather than rebuilding all relation source rows or hand-scanning family arrays locally.

`effect-target.ts` is the shared graph bridge from selected ontology target refs to associated effect contract ids. Target catalog, source-lowering preflight, source-lowering invocation, and source-lowering composition should use that helper rather than each projection inventing local effect joins. Associated effect ids are navigation/provenance, not proof that a generated fragment satisfies the contract. Effect targeting traverses promised-effect and participation/coordinating edges; input dependency edges remain missing-input facts and must not make raw input contracts look like effect promises. Detail projections that need direct witness facts, such as control-manifest-detail, should use direct manifest-witness helpers rather than the broad target graph.

Use `policy-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected scoped policy axes joined to input readiness and input contract detail. It is the policy drill-down, not recommendation evaluation, default selection, or source lowering.

Use `style-detail` after target selection when a caller needs selected styling mechanisms or visual policies joined to input readiness, visual-style input detail, coordinating application patterns, lower concept rows, and associated affordances. It is the style drill-down, not CSS generation, design taste, component library selection, or source lowering. Do not attach visual input dependencies to every styling mechanism row; direct mechanisms are selectable style/source values while style mechanism choice flows through the policy axis. `class-binding` is recommendable only in context of a selected pattern/control/visual policy that needs state-dependent classes or structural hooks; it is not a generated visual fallback or token source by itself.

Do not model package/build tooling as a styling mechanism. CSS Modules and some style mechanisms may require project-tooling facts, but those belong to SourcePlan project-tooling readiness or capability evidence, while styling-mechanism rows stay focused on style/source mechanisms.

Public app-builder answers can carry `targetAppBuilderQueryKind` and `targetAppBuilderQuery` continuations. Treat them as factual grouped drill-downs across catalog, readiness, detail, and part-source surfaces, not as recommendation ranking, source-policy selection, or adapter-local next-step prose. Transport-facing callers should use `SemanticRuntime.appBuilderQueryCatalog(...)` and `SemanticRuntime.answerAppBuilderQuery(...)` so query-claim wrapping and typed continuations are attached; pure helpers such as `answerSemanticRuntimeAppBuilderQuery(...)` are deterministic contract/fixture answerers unless explicitly wrapped by the shared continuation projector.

Generation is now a later phase. Add enough ontology/status/manifest breadth across controls, collections, style inputs, domain input, state policy, routing policy, and recommendation policy before treating any source-producing slice as proof. When decision bundles need canaries, start with source-placement/convention/resource policy and leaf controls such as native form controls rather than a whole starter-app profile.

Public app-builder rows should distinguish modeled, sourceLoweringImplemented, recommendation status, applicability/context, evidence, requires-explicit-input, and defaultingCandidate policy projection. Never use optional to mean source lowering is absent, and never treat defaultingCandidate as a blank-slate/new-app default. Request-local decision bundles are the current defaulting carrier; named profiles remain deferred until repeated bundles prove a stable shape. Compact answers should report decision-bundle counts by default and expose expansion rows only through detail/opt-in flags. Generated app-builder fixtures should prefer exact request-local decision bundles in `app-builder-request.json` and compact SourcePlan accounting in `app-builder-response.json`, while direct `suppliedInputs` remain a valid public readiness/input layer for callers that do not need grouped decision review; do not turn fixture metadata into a separate app-builder verification subsystem. The generated policy review compresses repeated applicability/evidence lane kinds as `kind xN` and expands focused rows into target-ref/facet detail; that multiplicity is review data, not a duplicate-row cleanup target by itself.

App-builder policy projection now starts in `packages/semantic-runtime/src/app-builder/policy`: recommendation-policy owns recommendation ranking, applicability rows, and evidence rows; defaulting-candidate-policy owns local fallback candidate scope/rationale rows; status-projection derives public source-lowering implementation from the executable registry. Row descriptors preserve declaredStatus for drift audits and projected status for public catalogs.

`target-catalog` rows now expose compact policy handles for menu use: `defaultingCandidate`, optional `defaultingCandidatePolicy` scope/rationale, and `policySatisfactionRequired` for contextual executable source-lowering targets. Exact target-catalog drill-downs preserve caller order; broad target-catalog calls use actionable-first menu order. Keep full applicability/evidence rows in `recommendation-policy` and policy-satisfaction state in `source-lowering-preflight`; target catalog should not become a hidden recommendation engine.

Use the public `recommendation-policy` query when an AI/operator needs reviewable recommendation/defaulting posture, applicability lanes, evidence lanes, compact counts, or contextual executable policy-satisfaction candidates. This query defaults to summary/counts and requires `includeRows: true` for the row table so MCP callers can inspect policy cheaply before drilling down. Exact target-ref recommendation-policy drill-downs preserve caller order; broad calls stay in ontology descriptor order. It does not select policy, invent defaults, or lower source; `policy-detail` remains the selected policy-axis/input-readiness drill-down.

Do not treat legacy `source-backed` authority as proof by itself. It is now a migration canary that should be sharpened into concrete framework/source/corpus/research/operator evidence when a row becomes important for generation; keep TBD rows review-visible instead of polishing uncertainty into confident prose.

Semantic-runtime should understand broadly while app-builder generates narrowly. Non-recommended, deferred, or analysis-only Aurelia/plugin forms remain visible in capability/detail answers without becoming default generated code.

Stable IDs are appropriate when they name a real selectable dimension. Use separate IDs for ontology targets, input contracts, source-lowering surfaces, source request fields, reusable patterns, caller/domain slots, seed data sets, Aurelia lowering axes, source plans, and verification effects when those choices need to be independently inspected, selected, or overridden.

Avoid recipe keys that smuggle multiple independent dimensions into one opaque name. Prefer a composition of typed selections when routing, validation, localization, service boundaries, seed data, domain shape, or presentation can vary independently.

Pattern identities name reusable mechanics such as app shells, collection browsing, state/domain boundaries, value channels, route selection, template-controller use, and class/style binding. They should not name sample domains, fixture copy, CSS, or starter records.

Domain presets are future explicit inputs, not current app-builder authority. Current source lowerers consume caller/AI supplied domain descriptors and seed records; if public presets return, they must materialize the same domain descriptor shape and remain selectable input, not reusable mechanic identity.

Sample domains and seed data should prove domain-slot swappability and seed-data policy without becoming example apps. Current pressure domains are only fixtures/default inputs; the reusable ontology remains collection, form, routing, state, value-channel, and source-plan mechanics.

Generated app fixture seed records should still read like plausible caller/domain data. Avoid fixture-verification, test-harness, or app-builder-internal wording in generated app source unless the fixture is explicitly analyzer pressure rather than end-user-shaped output. Caller-supplied structural data hooks should describe app/design roles, such as `data-au-role`, rather than internal generation provenance.

Current source lowerers consume explicit `SeedRecordSet` payloads and project `none` versus caller-supplied records; seed density/purpose selectors remain visible but deferred until a reviewed public preset/defaulting layer consumes them. Do not add `public-small`, demo, or inspection selectors as hidden record generators.

Generated route titles, headings, button text, labels, and other human-facing copy should be treated as caller/AI input or explicit source-naming/copy policy when the default derivation is not good enough. Do not hide route-copy taste inside routed lowerers; keep the generic-title canary visible until a reviewed copy/naming facet or defaulting policy exists.

Boolean domain fields are ordinary caller-authored fields unless an explicit future display/action policy supplies richer semantics. Do not promote the first boolean field to a status field, invent `Done/Open` labels, add `done` class hooks, or rename field labels to `Status` inside generic source lowerers.

Caller-supplied seed records should spend the input-contract-detail SeedRecordSet schema and AppBuilderSeedRecord vocabulary. The schema tells an AI what shape to provide; selected domain identity and field schemas decide whether a specific record is valid.

Domain relationship/action descriptors should spend AppBuilderDomainRelationshipKind, AppBuilderDomainActionKind, and AppBuilderDomainActionScope. Do not reintroduce local action or relationship string taxonomies in source lowerers.

CreateSubmitForm is read-only app-builder ontology terrain over domain command actions, native submit forms, domain-backed submit forms, native control binding, local view-model state, and DI state/domain classes. Treat it as first-ring form/action map data, not as a legacy draft-form lowerer, edit-buffer implementation, validation/i18n/plugin-store claim, or source-generation proof.

Source-lowering menus should expose required domain field schema expectations instead of hiding source-lowering limits in prose. If a selected target can only lower a subset of field value kinds or requires a minimum text/boolean/etc count, that requirement belongs on the domain slot and in the input/readiness contract.

Solution spaces and reference scenarios bias menus and fixture pressure. They may be opinionated about domains such as commerce, support, or analytics, but they should not carry concrete app nouns or sample records into pattern identity.

Aurelia lowering axes describe framework-specific projection choices after app intent and mechanics are known. App-global policies include convention admission, router admission, shared-state ownership, package capabilities, and app style policy.

Resource-local and area-local policies include resource kind, establishment carrier, and view form, local view-model or bindable state, binding-driven versus router-driven area navigation, resource DOM encapsulation, and resource style policy. Nested viewports are a consequence of router-driven area composition, not an independent app-global policy.

Before adding or reshaping durable Aurelia lowering axes, route through the framework capability terrain so source forms, locality, global policy, feature admission, and semantic consequences are not collapsed into one local enum.

The former resource-declaration axis was the canary for that terrain and is now split: app-convention policy stays the app/project gate, the resource-carrier axis holds the establishment forms (convention, decorator, static-$au, define-call, attribute-pattern-create) mirroring resources/ResourceCarrierKind, resource-kind mirrors ResourceDefinitionKind, and inline-vs-companion is the custom-element-view-form code-economy axis (custom-element-only).

Inline custom elements, Shadow DOM, cssModules, shadowCSS, global stylesheets, component stylesheets, local view-model state, bindable pass-through, and router-driven or binding-driven area selection are app-builder lowering axes now, but they should not be accepted by source generation until a compatible composition/source lowering can emit them truthfully.

Composition source support is per selected composition, not just per source-layout helper. A minimal shell should reject requested @aurelia/state ownership or validation admission, while the state-store collection and validated-form compositions may accept those choices because their source-plan callbacks actually emit the mechanics.

Future named profiles or blank-slate menus should be source-startability gated. Profiles can encode broader long-term ambitions, but the no-existing-app workflow should only advertise choices with at least one compatible SourcePlan lowerer under the current selection. Explicit unsupported selections can still return typed issues.

Source generation is a lowering step, not the ontology. Return typed menus, compatibility, required slots, preview files, and verification promises before emitting source text when that saves tokens or prevents accidental app-specific leakage.
Expected semantic effects are the verification contract for generated source. If a pattern composition cannot state what reopening the app should prove, its semantics are still too textual.

Routed collection/detail SourcePlan expected effects are grouped by app/state shape, route configuration source shape, route consumption/runtime reads, and template/runtime effects. Keep that split in `source-plan-expected-effects.ts` so app-builder generation, public SourcePlan previews, and fixture verification describe one semantic promise without hiding router expectations inside source-lowering code.

The retired recipe-authoring API is not a public generation surface. New app-generation work belongs in app-builder; migrated app-pattern fixtures in `fixtures/pressure/app-pattern-*` are pressure material, not an ontology to preserve.

Do not recreate catalog/storefront/table/form variants as hard-coded source generators. Model them as typed selections over ontology targets, input contracts, reusable patterns, caller/domain slots, seed data, Aurelia lowering axes, source plans, source-lowering surfaces, and expected effects.

Keep app-builder direction in Atlas memory and Work Router. Scratch `.temp` files may help a run, but durable routing should point at app-builder source files, pressure fixtures, and verification contracts.

Leaf control, binding-part, structural, binding-behavior, value-converter, framework-component, framework-syntax, framework-API, and resource-metadata catalogs may keep category-specific detail, but external consumers should enter through the unified part catalog. Resource-backed parts must remain mechanically checkable against `resources/built-in-resources.ts` instead of relying on prose or display syntax; framework API parts should cite exported API names/module specifiers and source serializers owned by the semantic-runtime package that models the framework behavior.

App-builder part syntax is a cue, not an authority. Durable part rows should expose resource/capability facts, application sites, source-operation family, required slots, optional slots, value channels, and package requirements so future lowerers can operate on typed selections instead of string templates.

The dense part-source gallery is fixture-pressure for public part-source sample coverage. Keep `appBuilderPartSourceGallerySourcePlanFrame`, `appBuilderPartSourceGallerySourcePlanAssembly`, `galleryAppSourceFrame`, and the gallery source-section helpers as generated fixture scaffolding, not app-builder recommendation policy, starter profile material, or a second source-generation API.

Do not re-declare built-in resource identity inside app-builder. Resource-backed app-builder metadata should point at semantic-runtime built-in resource refs, then add app-builder-specific summaries, operation families, application sites, slots, value-channel consequences, and business rules on top.

Do not re-declare built-in binding-command identity inside app-builder. Syntax-backed binding parts should point at semantic-runtime built-in syntax refs, then add app-builder-specific summaries, slots, value-channel consequences, and source-lowering rules on top. State `.state` and `.dispatch` are syntax-backed state commands, not generic callback conventions.

State-plugin source lowerers must respect Aurelia's store-scope boundary. Inside `repeat.for="item of items & state"`, ordinary item locals are outside `.state`/`.dispatch` expression scope; use `$parent.item.id` deliberately for dispatch payload identities, and use read-only ordinary bindings such as `checked.to-view` when store item display should not mutate the repeated object directly.

Record-shaped state-plugin entities cannot own class accessors. When finite choices, dates, or choice sets need display text, emit exported domain display helper functions from the store and expose them through the generated view-model instead of forcing class semantics onto immutable store records.

Do not force framework TypeScript APIs into template-resource categories. APIs such as `@fromState(...)`, `@computed(...)`, and `AppTask.*(...)` are framework-API parts with TypeScript source fragments and import requirements; their source text should be serialized by the state/observation/configuration/router/DI/etc package that already owns the framework semantics.

Framework API app-builder parts cite exported API names plus module specifiers directly. Do not route these APIs through the built-in resource package catalog just because some APIs are admitted through StandardConfiguration; framework APIs, resources, compiler syntax, and resource metadata are separate source-lowering lanes.

Do not force compiler-special template syntax into resource or binding-command categories. Framework syntax parts such as `as-element` and usage-site `containerless` should point at `template/special-attribute-source.ts` so lookup and source serialization stay with the template compiler substrate.

Do not force resource definition metadata into template syntax or exported framework API categories. Resource-metadata parts such as `dependencies: [...]` should point at `resources/resource-definition-source.ts` so generated source stays aligned with resource-definition convergence and local dependency visibility.

Do not force component view-model lifecycle methods into framework API, decorator, or template categories. Component-lifecycle parts such as `attached()` and `detaching()` should point at `template/component-lifecycle-source.ts` so TypeScript class-member generation stays aligned with runtime-html Controller hook discovery.

Built-in binding command names are `BuiltInBindingCommandName` values in the template substrate. App-builder lowerers should spend those enum values through built-in syntax refs or `builtInBindingCommandAttributeText(...)` instead of reintroducing command-name string literals or local `target.command` formatters.

Common authored target names used by framework-owned binding commands are `BuiltInBindingCommandTargetName` values in the template substrate. App-builder controls and binding parts should spend that vocabulary for `value`, `checked`, `element`, `class`, `style`, `model`, `matcher`, and `repeat`, then use caller slots for genuinely user-authored target names.

Source slots should keep their semantic form through lowering. `switch-case` currently accepts a binding-expression slot and therefore lowers to `case.bind="expr"`; the framework also supports plain literal `case="value"`, but that requires a separate literal-valued slot if app-builder needs to offer it.

App-builder part value channels must distinguish part-owned channels from target-observer-resolved channels. Controls and specific syntax such as `token.class` own their channel at part selection time; generic `ATTRIBUTE.bind` / `ATTRIBUTE.to-view` only name a target and expression until the fragment is attached to a concrete host element and semantic-runtime runs target-access, ObserverLocator, and value-channel materialization.

Use the app-builder part menu when an AI/MCP caller needs to discover applicable parts by source locus, operation family, exact part identity, resource kind/package, package dependency, slot availability, or slot value-language availability. Do not make callers join individual category catalogs by hand.

Keep the public app-builder part menu compact by default. Menu rows should carry enough stable identity, source-locus, slot, value-channel, and dependency facts for a caller to choose a part; descriptor-heavy category detail is opt-in through `includePartDetails`, and internal source-lowering code should use `appBuilderPartMenuDescriptors(...)` instead of relying on transport rows.

Use `appBuilderPartMenuAxisSummary` for compact part-menu aggregate facts such as part kinds, tiers, application sites, slot expectations, resource identities, and package dependencies. Do not recompute those rollups in display text, preview, MCP adapters, or fixture helpers.

App-builder is intentionally opinionated. `framework.capabilities` is the broad map of what Aurelia can do; app-builder should default to the preferred robust subset and expose intent-scoped or advanced parts only when caller intent, package/resource-package selection, exact part ids, explicit authoring-tier selection, or existing-app context asks for them.

Do not treat full semantic-runtime support as app-builder recommendation scope. Semantic-runtime should understand every important Aurelia form for analysis, diagnostics, fixtures, and existing-app support; app-builder should write the smaller maintainable subset that keeps generated apps compact and navigable for AI agents with limited context windows.

Recommendation policy must be contextual rather than a fixed heuristic. Future app-builder preference profiles should account for caller taste such as framework-heavy compact Aurelia, web-standards-forward code, large-scale service/domain boundaries, low-file-count starters, and existing-project conventions before deciding which supported forms to advertise or lower.

Treat app-builder to framework-capability reconciliation findings as triage, not lint. Missing capability ids are risks; mixed source role and scope spread are review points; taste-only values are acceptable when app-builder names policy or code-economy choices with explicit rationale.

Use `AppBuilderPartAuthoringTier` to keep default menus from advertising every modeled framework nook. The tier is recommendation policy, not semantic-runtime support: advanced parts may still have executable lowerers, pressure fixtures, and framework-capability mappings without becoming default generated-code advice. Shape filters such as part kind, source locus, operation family, or slot availability should narrow the preferred subset rather than implicitly widen into all supported forms.

Public part-menu and source-lowering-preview answers carry `authoringTierPolicy`. Use that field to explain whether the answer stayed on the default preferred subset, used explicit authoring tiers, or widened because the caller supplied exact part/package/resource-package intent; do not make MCP callers infer that gate from display prose.

Shape-only app-builder part requests may match supported non-preferred rows while returning no default rows. Keep `authoringTierFilteredOutCount` and its summary on part-menu and source-lowering-preview answers so AI callers can see the capability exists without treating it as default generated-code advice.

Zero-row part-menu answers with `authoringTierFilteredOutCount` should continue to a deliberately widened `part-menu` query, not directly to `part-source-lowering-preview`. The widened menu is the review step where an AI can inspect the non-preferred rows and decide whether advanced or intent-scoped parts are appropriate.

Current source-lowering previews carry `effectContractIds` into effect-contract detail. Follow those ids for SourcePlan/reopen witness descriptors, public app-query rows, and expected-effect product terrain; do not treat legacy expected-effect previews as the only proof vocabulary.

Use the app-builder part menu's package-dependency filter when a caller asks for parts that require a package such as `@aurelia/i18n`, `@aurelia/state`, router, validation-html, ui-virtualization, or `aurelia` itself. Some package-backed parts are syntax-backed or framework-API-backed rather than resource-backed, so `packageDependencies` is the truthful filter when resource-package identity is not the right axis.

Use the app-builder part menu's slot value-language filters when the caller knows it can provide source values such as Aurelia binding expressions, TypeScript expressions, router instructions, or AppTask slot names but should not hand-map those languages back to every slot kind. Slot kinds remain the stable invocation keys, and each menu part row carries resolved required/optional slot expectations.

Use `appBuilderPartSlotExpectation(...)` when a concrete part is known. Global slot descriptors describe caller keys; part slot expectations refine those keys through framework syntax such as `.state` using an Aurelia function-entry value while `.dispatch` keeps an ordinary property-entry value.

Pattern compositions may declare part uses when a higher-order mechanic spends concrete controls, binding parts, template controllers, binding behaviors, value converters, or framework components. Treat those declarations as composition metadata and verification pressure, not as the final source-lowering mechanism.

Part source lowering is the executable callback layer for part rows: stable part kind/id plus optional application site plus typed slot assignments lower to source fragments and package dependencies. Source plans should spend those callbacks instead of hand-writing syntax that a lowerer already owns.

Keep the source invocation/result contract separate from executable lowerers and sample policy. `part-source-invocation.ts` owns transportable states/fragments/issues, `part-source-lowering.ts` owns executable callbacks, and `part-source-samples.ts` owns AI-facing proof/preview samples.

Primitive control callbacks such as `choiceInputGroupElement(...)` and `selectElement(...)` belong to `part-source-lowering.ts` and should stay descriptor/slot driven. Higher-order form or collection composition should delegate to these executable part-source callbacks rather than reintroducing local checkbox/radio/select template strings.

Direct part-source invocation fragments carry compact origin facts naming the selected part kind/id, operation kind, application site, and spent slot kinds. Keep that provenance on invocation results for later composition, diagnostics, and IDE/MCP explanations; omit repeated origin details from compact preview rows unless a caller asks for the concrete invocation result.

Generated SourcePlans should preserve app-builder source origins through `SourcePlanContribution`, not just final text. Direct part fragments carry `AppBuilderPartSourceInvocation` origins, one-target source-lowering fragments carry `AppBuilderSourceLoweringInvocation` origins, and multi-fragment source-lowering wrappers carry `AppBuilderSourceLoweringComposition` origins naming the exact app-builder ontology target. TypeScript preludes, HTML templates, controls, binding parts, structural parts, router/i18n/state/validation resources, source-lowering wrappers/buttons/messages/forms, and framework API fragments should remain inspectable by MCP/IDE callers through compact source-plan contribution facts.

Generated TypeScript preludes should accept origin-bearing source contributions when those fragments own import requirements. Do not first project fragments to anonymous import requirements and then append the original fragments again; use source-plan TypeScript source assembly so rendered imports and provenance rows spend the same contribution ledger.

Use `source-plan-contributions.ts` and `template-source-plan.ts` when moving app-builder fragments into generated files. A template source lowerer that has part fragments should return HTML plus fragments, then convert that pair into a SourcePlanFile instead of flattening known origins into string-only templates.

Keep the app-builder part-source gallery contribution-aware. It is the dense executable callback pressure fixture, so new part lowerers should prove generated syntax, TypeScript import requirements, type-only import boundaries, and source-plan provenance there. Its coverage check must compare every public part-source sample invocation shape against SourcePlan contribution origins, and `catalog-integrity` should expose those issues so MCP/Atlas callers do not have to run fixture materialization to discover drift. Expected gallery shapes stay enum-backed app-builder identities; observed SourcePlan origin identities stay raw neutral strings until a coverage issue explains drift. Keep the app-builder source-lowering gallery contribution-aware too: it should materialize every sourceLoweringImplemented target row through source-lowering invocation/composition/direct SourcePlan preview, and catalog-integrity should expose missing or unexpected ontology target coverage.

Executable source callbacks receive `AppBuilderPartSlotReader`, the per-invocation reader for the selected part and its validated slots. Add typed slot readers there when a new lowerer needs closed values or richer source forms; do not pass bare maps deeper into new callback layers.

Public part-source invocation should explain bad caller selections. Unknown kind/id pairs should return the `unknown-part` lowering state with issues; known cataloged parts that have no executable callback should return `unsupported-part`.

Public part-source invocation validates caller slots before callback execution. Duplicate slots, unsupported slots, malformed Aurelia expressions, invalid TypeScript fragments, invalid HTML/event names, and unknown AppTask slots should return slot-language-aware `invalid-slot-assignments` or `missing-slots` issues rather than escaping as callback failures or late generated-source mismatches.

App-builder part IDs are enum-backed through the category catalogs and projected as `AppBuilderPartId`. Keep `AppBuilderPartKind` beside the ID because category IDs can overlap; do not fall back to anonymous strings for durable part identity.

Part source-lowering integrity must check generated syntax, not only registry coverage. Required-only and optional-slot sample invocations should parse generated template attributes through `parseBuiltInAttributeSyntax(...)` and compare resource-backed elements/attributes with built-in resource facts.

Part source-lowering integrity must also check generated fragment families against advertised application sites. A source callback that returns a template element for a binding-command-target part, or an attribute for a template-element part, is a source-lowering contract bug.

Atlas app-builder reconciliation has separate lowering-axis, part-backed, source-plan-backed, and substrate-backed capability lanes. Do not force checked/select/class-style/template-controller/router-resource/framework-API rows into app-global policy axes when the source-derived part catalog already covers them; do not force nested route declarations or route-recognizer use into router admission when the routed SourcePlan lowerer already covers them; do not force StandardConfiguration, RouterConfiguration, actionless form submit prevention, or StateDefaultConfiguration admission into source-shape policy axes when generated entrypoint, source-plan, or substrate lowerers already cover them.

`appBuilderPartSourceLoweringPreview(...)` is the reviewable bridge between app-builder part menus and source generation. Use it when a caller needs callable sample invocations, fragment kinds, structural template hints, package dependencies, and optional source text without generating an app. Use `part-source-invocation` / `lowerAppBuilderPartSourceInvocation(...)` when a caller has selected one concrete part and supplied slots.

Source-lowering preview samples are AI-facing examples, not executable framework facts. They should be grounded in framework source/docs/tests and in app-builder part semantics; do not use generic `value`/`arg` placeholders when a part has a more truthful source shape such as a state action object or i18n options object. Keep sample resource names neutral placeholders such as `sample-card`/`SampleCard`; generated fixture domains and seed records belong in generated fixture requests and manifests, not in reusable part-source sample policy.
Treat `GeneratedSyntaxMismatch` as a source-lowering substrate canary. It usually means a lowerer emitted display-like text that no longer agrees with the semantic-runtime syntax/resource catalogs.

Treat `GeneratedFragmentSiteMismatch` as the neighboring source-lowering canary. It usually means catalog application-site metadata and executable source callbacks no longer describe the same lowering locus.

Control rows should split native binding target names from built-in binding-command syntax refs. Avoid `value.bind`/`checked.bind` as durable control keys; the target is app-builder metadata and `.bind` is framework syntax. Simple native controls should also carry structured source element skeletons so source lowerers do not re-declare tag/type details or pre-rendered static attributes beside the catalog.

Choice controls should keep option-domain mechanics in typed source slots: required selected-value and option-domain slots, plus optional local-name, option-value-expression, option-binding-kind, option-label-expression, and matcher-expression slots. Lowerers should serialize repeat/model-or-value/label/matcher syntax through shared template/binding-command helpers, not through ad hoc select/input markup.

Generated command-bearing attributes should parse their raw value through `builtInBindingCommandExpressionType(...)` so app-builder lowerers reuse the same property/function/iterator entry family as the framework-shaped binding-command build path.

Binding-expression fragments emitted for binding behaviors and value converters should parse through `ExpressionParser` and compare with the resource-backed part name. Structured template-element fragments should expose nested attributes and mixed text/element child nodes so compound controls do not hide source syntax in raw multiline strings.

Template-attribute fragments should carry `AuthoredTemplateAttributeSource` beside rendered text. Composition and source-plan lowerers should append or inspect that structured source instead of reparsing `name="value"` text after a part lowerer has already emitted the attribute.

Event listener parts should cover both framework-owned listener command forms. `event-listener` spends `BuiltInBindingCommandName.Trigger`; `event-capture-listener` spends `BuiltInBindingCommandName.Capture`. Add future listener variants through built-in syntax refs rather than local command strings.

App-builder source-lowering helpers are the shared invocation layer for common controls, template attributes, template controllers, state commands, structured template-element source, seed-record literals, and generated name normalization. Generated name normalization in that helper layer adapts the shared `source-plan/source-name.ts` substrate; do not add independent casing, word-splitting, or pluralization rules inside app-builder lowerers. New source lowerers should reuse that helper layer before creating local helper forks or hand-writing tags that a shared source composer can own.

Generic authored-template and binding-expression serialization belongs in `template/authored-template-source.ts` and `template/binding-expression-source.ts`, not in app-builder. App-builder should spend those primitives for static attributes, plain text content, structured elements, mixed text/element child nodes, interpolation holes, iterator headers, binding-behavior modifiers, and value-converter modifiers.

Router resource part lowerers should spend `route-instruction-source.ts` rather than formatting `load` or `href` strings locally. `load` can use framework inline multi-binding slots for route, params, context, active state, and target attribute; `href` stays a no-multi-binding custom attribute. `au-viewport` source lowering should expose the framework bindables (`name`, `usedBy`, `default`, `fallback`) through slots instead of app-builder-local tag strings.

I18n translation source lowerers should spend built-in i18n syntax refs and app-builder source callbacks for `t`, `t.bind`, and `t-params.bind`. Future localized generated source should also use source-plan i18n configuration admission so generated translation resources reopen as i18n translation-key and TranslationBinding products.

Validation-html source lowerers should spend validation framework-component callbacks rather than raw custom-attribute text. Future validation generated source should prefer `validation-errors.from-view` when errors flow from the validation custom attribute back into the view-model collection; the framework also accepts `validation-errors.bind` through the two-way bindable default, but that is not the recommended generated-source channel.

Route config/decorator source belongs in router-owned source helpers, not in app-builder generators. App-builder routed compositions should spend those helpers plus router-resource part invocations so route config, `load`, route-context reads, and viewport source stay aligned with semantic-runtime router products.

`IRouteContext.getRouteParameters(...)` is a framework API part, not generated-route-local TypeScript text. Source lowerers should spend `route-context-parameter-read` with a typed route-parameter type slot, optional merge strategy, optional query inclusion, and import requirements so generated route reads remain traceable to router capability rows.

Routed source-pattern parameters are executable source inputs, not advisory copy. `list-route-path` and `detail-route-parameter` must move through route config, router `load` links, route-context read source, reflected SourcePattern source values, expected-effect filters, and pressure fixtures; if any one of those stays at the default, fix the source-lowering contract.

Framework-owned source forms belong beside their semantic-runtime framework model, not inside app-builder. Template controllers spend `template-controller-source.ts`; `au-compose`, `au-slot`, and `portal` spend template-owned source helpers; `au-viewport` spends router-owned `viewport-source.ts`; validation-html display resources spend validation-owned `validation-html-source.ts`.

Built-in resource bindables belong in `resources/built-in-resource-bindables.ts`. Built-in full-definition materialization, app-builder resource callbacks, runtime composition, and validation/router source helpers should spend that shared vocabulary instead of carrying parallel bindable-name tables.

Atlas app-builder reconciliation must resolve framework-owned enum and const resource names. If moving a part catalog from raw literals to semantic-runtime constants makes capability coverage disappear, fix the Atlas extractor rather than reintroducing app-builder-local strings.

Composition ids are stable enum-backed selections (`AppBuilderCompositionId`), not anonymous strings. New source-plan callbacks should register against that enum and keep duplicate/orphan coverage in `appBuilderCompositionSourceLoweringCatalogIssues`.

Framework/plugin entrypoint admission belongs in source-plan. App-builder source lowerers should consume `AureliaConfigurationAdmissionSource` helpers for RouterConfiguration, StateDefaultConfiguration, I18nConfiguration, ValidationHtmlConfiguration, and future admissions instead of local import/dependency/registration strings.

App-builder source-starting lowerers should return SourcePlan projectTooling when generated app source is meant to be runnable. Package dependencies, scripts, package manifests, tsconfig, and local asset declarations belong in SourcePlanProjectTooling rather than being hidden as ordinary source files.

App-builder source-starting lowerers that create multiple generated files should use `AppBuilderSourcePlanAssembly` so app-builder baseline policy, generated text authority, configured entrypoint admission, dependency evidence, and project tooling are carried by one generation transaction instead of repeated local constructor blocks.

Generated SourcePlans should carry SourcePattern metadata whenever source text is complete. SourcePattern explains whether the lowered source is domain-neutral or caller-applied, whether seed data is caller-supplied/generated/fixture data, and which reusable source-pattern modules were spent; it is not a replacement for app-builder pattern composition or expected semantic effects. Routed browse/detail source patterns spend caller-supplied domain and SeedRecordSet input, so their data policy should be caller-supplied rather than generated sample data.

Use `AppBuilderSourcePatternKey` for app-builder-owned source-pattern identities instead of raw string keys. The key should track the emitted source shape (for example minimal convention shell versus explicit-resource shell), while pattern compositions remain the higher-order menu choices.

The Phase 1 app-builder ontology skeleton now lives under `packages/semantic-runtime/src/app-builder/ontology/*`. `ontology-catalog` exposes input contracts, policy axes, effect contracts, affordances, application patterns, collection concepts, control patterns, styling mechanisms, visual policies, and honest status without lowering source.

Use scoped app-builder policy axes for convention admission, router admission, area navigation, state ownership, local state, resource carrier, custom-element view form, DOM encapsulation, styling mechanism, and plugin admission. Do not collapse app-global, resource-local, area-local, component-local, and control-local choices into one co-equal enum.

Control-pattern detail has explicit selection, readiness, row, and summary phase frames. Future control inspection work should reuse `controlPatternDetailSelectionFrame`, `controlPatternDetailReadinessFrame`, `controlPatternDetailRowsFrame`, and `controlPatternDetailSummaryFrame` instead of folding include flags, readiness, joins, and compact counts back into the public endpoint. `inputContractDetailsForControlPattern` returns no detail rows for empty contract sets rather than opening the full input-contract catalog.

`ontology-catalog` accepts read-only filters for ontology domains and status/policy fields (`recommendationStatuses`, `reasonAuthorities`, `sourceLoweringImplemented`, `defaultingCandidate`, `requiresExplicitInput`) so MCP/IDE callers can keep context small without inventing new query families.

`ontology-catalog` is summary-first by default. Compact answers expose domain summaries, `rowCount`, `sourceLoweringImplementedCount`, `relationCount`, and display flags while keeping full row-family arrays and relation rows empty. Request `includeRows: true` for row detail and `includeRelations: true` or `relationKinds` for graph edges; filtered catalog reads should remain compact unless detail is explicitly requested.

`target-catalog` also accepts `reasonAuthorities` so selectable ontology rows can be narrowed to operator-confirmed, source-backed, or TBD status terrain without treating catalog-integrity as the only review queue.

`appBuilderTargetCatalog` is the selectable-target read model between the raw ontology map and per-target readiness. It returns ontology row refs with honest status and compact readiness counts, supports paging and row/status/readiness filters, and should be used when an AI needs candidate targets without source generation. Exact targetRefs remain the internal lossless selector and preserve caller ordering; compact targetSelectors let public callers provide kind/id and have semantic-runtime derive/check the domain centrally. When target selection is omitted, the row order is intentionally actionable-first so executable app-builder source-lowering targets appear before broad input/policy/detail terrain. The no-argument target-catalog answer returns a 25-row first page for MCP token economy; filtered, exact, and caller-paged requests use shared public paging behavior.

`sourceLoweringImplemented` means the exact ontology row can be invoked through app-builder source-lowering. Related input, policy, effect, or manifest rows may be selected and joined through detail queries but should remain sourceLoweringImplemented=false unless invocation supports that row kind directly; use source-lowering consumer/detail rows when an input facet is consumed by an executable target.

`status-audit.ts` owns catalog-integrity review over the app-builder status matrix. Hard contradictions such as sourceLoweringImplemented without a registered surface are integrity issues; TBD/provisional rows are review-needed rows so MCP/IDE callers can see uncertainty without treating it as failed registry integrity. Catalog-integrity answers with status-audit rows should continue to exact `target-catalog` rows, `recommendation-policy` rows, and family-specific detail queries for those same targets; the integrity probe is a recovery surface, not a policy selector. Use `reasonAuthorities` filters on ontology-catalog or target-catalog when a caller needs a direct TBD/source-backed/operator-confirmed menu.

`readSemanticRuntimeAppBuilderCatalogIntegrity` is the public catalog-integrity assembler. Keep registry checks, generated gallery coverage, and status/recommendation policy audit in `semanticRuntimeAppBuilderCatalogRegistryIssueFrame`, `semanticRuntimeAppBuilderCatalogGalleryCoverageFrame`, and `semanticRuntimeAppBuilderCatalogPolicyAuditFrame` so the recovery surface stays compact without hiding each issue authority.

`appBuilderSourceLoweringInvocation` is the source-lowering-implemented generated-fragment bridge after preflight. It lowers one exact ontology target with explicit supplied inputs, reports deterministic field-selection state, and delegates template syntax to part-source callbacks instead of inventing target-local strings.
