Current source-lowering-implemented source invocation support is deliberately narrow but now covers scalar native controls, native choice controls, native button event controls, form-message fragments, inline field-group composition, and DomainCommandAction class-member fragments. Scalar controls lower from domain field payloads to value/checked fragments; choice controls spend field-local options, a selected reusable domain value set, or an explicit valueDomainExpression and report value-domain provenance; native buttons spend domain actions plus accessibility labels, explicit/defaulted native buttonType, and delegate click/event syntax through the event-listener part lowerer; form messages spend accessibility-help-error payloads or explicit message text/kind and report message-selection provenance; field groups wrap a derived or explicitly selected native field control with label and help/error/status relationships while reporting inner-control, label-text, field-control-id, and described-by provenance. `AccessibilityHelpError.fieldName` scopes help/error/status payloads to one generated field group; unscoped payloads remain target-global for standalone/simple cases. Native Submit Form composition reports stale field-scoped `ControlAccessibility` or `VisualClassHooks` inputs as typed issues when their `fieldName` is not in the selected form fields, and reports submit-button `VisualClassHooks` scoped to an action other than the selected submit action instead of silently dropping the hook. Radio-group and checkbox-list controls use fieldset/legend source shape in both direct standalone invocation and embedded field groups rather than repeated id/for relationships on option labels; FieldControl visual hooks attach to descendant repeated input elements, while FieldGroup visual hooks own embedded fieldset wrappers. Current field-group source derives static DOM ids from selected field names unless the caller supplies fieldControlId; this is acceptable for current root-level generated fixtures, but reusable component generation needs an explicit identity policy before treating static ids as universally recommendable. DomainCommandAction spends explicit `actionName` and optional caller-owned `methodParameters`; `methodBodyStatements` remain caller-owned except for narrow first-ring local create and entity-complete actions derived from complete domain, field/action, numeric identity or typed row parameter, and explicit local collection input. Explicit mutating entity actions can mark caller-declared `inputFieldNames` writable in generated local collection entity source; this supports caller-authored update/archive/assign command bodies without making app-builder invent those bodies. App-builder selects syntax and provenance but does not invent arbitrary domain behavior or infer return types from arbitrary method body text. If generated commands need explicit return types, add a modeled method-signature input. Direct invocation fields that carry accessibility facts are normalized into explicit caller accessibility facets before preflight so the gate and lowerer share one input model. Source-lowering request selector fields such as buttonType, messageKind, innerControlPatternId, fieldControlSelections, actionName, methodParameters, and methodBodyStatements should stay enum-backed; untyped transport calls that supply unknown selectors report raw rejected text on issue requested* fields while candidate/selected fields remain typed vocabulary values. VisualClassHooks is now a modeled visual-style payload that source-lowering invocation and composition can spend as caller/AI supplied class tokens and data-* attributes for form, field-group, label, field-control, field-message, and button targets; app-builder template-element source composition merges generated class tokens and duplicate data-* attributes when multiple modeled hooks land on one generated element. Absence remains a missing/recommended visual input state, not permission to invent CSS. Rich controls, component wrappers, standalone/full submit workflow semantics outside Native Submit Form, visual tokens/CSS/design-system payloads, and state-dispatch actions remain non-executable until their action, accessibility, manifest, style, or realization policies are modeled as spendable inputs.

Generated `ControlUseInventory` now has a concrete app-builder source-lowering product: invocation answers emit `controlUseInventoryRows`, composition answers aggregate member rows plus direct composition-owned rows for controls such as sortable table header buttons and native submit buttons, and SourcePlan preview answers carry those generated rows at the file-review boundary. Rows preserve generated source references, inline-native realization, control pattern / leaf-control identity, field/action facts, binding or event expressions, button/message/accessibility facts, fragment kinds, and action-channel kind (`direct-control-event` versus `containing-form-submit`). Existing authored-source inventory, file-span attachment, wrapper/external manifests, and extraction/edit decisions remain future terrain. Do not flip effect-contract or manifest-row `sourceLoweringImplemented` for this; that bit is reserved for exact callable app-builder source-lowering targets.

`appBuilderSourceLoweringComposition` is the source-lowering-implemented generated-fragment bridge for one selected ontology target that owns several member invocations or structural/binding parts. It currently supports `CollectionList`, `CollectionCard`, `CollectionTable`, `LoadingEmptyErrorState`, and `NativeSubmitForm`. Collection list/card/table spend explicit `collectionExpression`, `itemLocalName`, collection display/table payloads, repeat, and text-interpolation parts; they do not invent data sources, selection/service-query mechanics, empty-state conditions, CSS, or sample domains. `selectCollectionProjectionFrame` owns the shared collection-projection setup: target-scoped supplied-input expansion, collection binding context validation, optional empty-state/else branch lowering, available field/action names, and collection display/table payload summaries. Reuse that frame for list/card/table setup while keeping each renderer's source shape distinct. `collectionTableRenderedFrame` owns table-specific repeat/header/body/table fragment assembly, empty-state sibling fragments, visual hooks, member target ids, and control-use rows; do not hide those table-only action/sort/filter/pagination semantics behind a generic collection renderer. Collection Table can lower explicit row action columns from `CollectionTableColumns.actionName` plus matching `DomainActions`; TypeScript-safe action names default to `actionName(itemLocalName)`, while custom or unsafe handler expressions require `actionHandlerExpressions`. It can lower sortable field columns only when the caller supplies exactly one matching `sortHandlerExpressions` row; the generated header button delegates click syntax through the event-listener part lowerer. It can lower filterable field columns only when the caller supplies matching `filterBindingExpressions`; generated local view-model collection state can emit the corresponding filter member and derived filtered-collection getter when `CollectionQueryFeatures` selects local filtering. It can lower local pagination when the caller supplies exactly one `CollectionQueryFeatures` local-pagination row with `pageSize`, generated local view-model page-state source, and explicit pagination control request fields. Source-lowering now reads `CollectionQueryFeatures`: local sorting, local filtering, local pagination, and first-ring scalar row selection are the query rungs it can spend. Row selection requires explicit `CollectionIdentityPolicy` plus table checked/toggle/label request fields. Batch actions are now first-ring local collection-scoped toolbar buttons when explicit `BatchActions`, `RowSelection`, scalar `CollectionIdentityPolicy`, and `batchActionControls` are supplied; select-all, cross-page/service-backed selection, richer identity policies, and broad service-backed sort/pagination/totals query still block with typed issues until their lowerers and policies exist; narrow component-pair service filtering and text search live in service collection support rather than collection-table projection. Current local sort method bodies remain caller-owned through DomainCommandAction because comparison policy, direction, in-place/query-state policy, and active-sort state are not yet modeled source inputs. NativeSubmitForm can spend optional `fieldControlSelections` to pass explicit per-field inner control-pattern ids into field-group invocations when value-kind defaults are insufficient; composition validates selected fields and enum-backed control-pattern ids, rejects field-scoped accessibility/visual payloads whose `fieldName` is outside the selected form fields, then leaves compatibility/value-domain checks to the lower-level field-group invocation. LoadingEmptyErrorState can render an optional explicit nested `fulfilledContentComposition` in the fulfilled non-empty branch and carries nested target refs, control-use rows, and issue bridging through the parent result; this is structured composition nesting, not permission to invent async data sources or lifecycle semantics. This is native-button/event-listener and template-controller projection support, not service/state/router action realization.

LoadingEmptyErrorState spends the caller-supplied `promiseExpression` exactly. Generated app fixtures should pair it with the explicit `AsyncDataSource` source-lowering target when they need a stable promise-valued member; do not add a hidden promise-property invention inside the loading/empty/error lowerer.

Collection list/card/table and routed browse/detail list-route generated source should spend explicit empty-state conditions as branch control flow: the empty message receives `if.bind` and the collection surface receives sibling `else`. Do not leave an empty table/list/card or routed list visible beside the empty message unless a future explicit display policy asks for that.

`APP_BUILDER_SOURCE_LOWERING_COMPOSITION_TARGET_ROWS` is the exact-target registry for FragmentComposition source-lowering. Keep it synchronized with the source-lowering surface registry and composition lowerer map instead of adding target-specific conditionals.

App-builder source-lowering invocation and composition answers expose `sourceLoweringTargetRefs` plus associated `effectContractIds`. Continuations should spend those fields to offer selected ontology detail and `effect-contract-detail` without parsing generated fragments or relying on adapter-local heuristics.

Collection List, Collection Card, and Collection Table are source-lowerable ApplicationPattern rows for projection-only fragments. They require domain fields and collection projection payloads at preflight, then require explicit collection expression and item local name at composition time. CollectionDisplayFields is field-backed projection terrain only; command/action placement belongs in action-aware inputs such as CollectionTableColumns.actionName plus DomainActions. Collection Table row actions are now a narrow projection feature when the caller supplies explicit action columns and domain actions. CollectionTableColumns.sortable/filterable are modeled table/query metadata; sortable field columns have an explicit-handler lowering rung through `sortHandlerExpressions`, and filterable field columns have an explicit-binding/local-query-state rung through `filterBindingExpressions` plus generated local view-model filter state. Basic table projection still does not require `CollectionQueryFeatures`, but when callers supply that facet, preflight/composition enforce it: local sorting requires sortable field-backed columns, local filtering requires filterable field-backed columns, and local pagination requires exactly one page-size feature row plus explicit pagination control request fields. Local scalar row selection is now a narrow source-lowerable rung when explicit identity policy and request fields are supplied. Batch actions are now a narrow first-ring local toolbar rung when explicit selection, scalar identity policy, and `batchActionControls` are supplied. Select-all, richer identity policies, broad service-backed sort/pagination/totals query, loading/error lifecycle depth, and service/state/router action realization remain modeled/deferred adjacent work rather than hidden fragment lowering; narrow service filtering/text search is intentionally handled by component-pair service collection support.

AppShell is a source-lowerable ApplicationPattern row through direct SourcePlan preview, not through fragment invocation/composition. It emits only the minimal non-routed root shell, requires explicit source root, app name, resource carrier, and convention policy, and rejects convention/policy contradictions. Routed app shell support should be modeled as its own target rather than re-expanding the minimal AppShell row.

Native Submit Form is a source-lowerable ApplicationPattern row. It requires domain fields and domain actions at preflight, then requires explicit field order, selected action, submit button text, and any unsafe binding/handler overrides at composition time. Choice value-set input belongs to selected choice member controls, not to every scalar submit form globally.

Treat source-lowering composition as fragments plus provenance, not a host write or full app generator. Top-level form fragments carry source-lowering composition origins, child field/control fragments carry source-lowering invocation origins, and delegated submit syntax carries part-source origin so SourcePlan can later explain every generated layer.

The public app-builder semantic-runtime surface is `SemanticRuntime.appBuilderQueryCatalog(...)` plus `SemanticRuntime.answerAppBuilderQuery(...)` in `api/app-builder.ts`. Keep this separate from app-world query kinds: app-builder menus, part source invocation, and SourcePlan generation do not open an app.

The app-builder query catalog and answerer registry are one checked API surface. Adding a query kind must update the enum, catalog row, and answerer together so MCP/IDE transports never advertise an uncallable app-builder query.

Public app-builder query catalog rows should advertise accepted request fields and source-lowering request fields through enum-backed metadata. AI callers should learn fields such as `targetCatalog`, `sourceLoweringPreflight`, `sourceLoweringSourcePlan`, `partMenu`, `partSourceLoweringPreview`, and `partSourceInvocation` from the catalog rather than stale examples or display prose.

Public app-builder query catalog rows also carry `SemanticRuntimeAppBuilderQueryPosture`. Use it to distinguish the ontology read model from source-lowering surfaces, reusable part-source substrate, integrity probes, and the surface map; query availability alone is not app-builder ontology authority.

The app-builder ontology catalog carries `AppBuilderOntologyRelationRow` graph edges. Use those edges for input-dependency, promised-effect, uses-application-pattern, pattern-to-concept, and follow-up-affordance joins instead of asking callers or future maintainers to reconstruct dependencies from prose row summaries.

Use `has-input-facet` relation rows to explain what an input contract contains. Keep facet rows on the read-only ontology path so an AI can ask for domain fields, source layout, visual hooks, collection columns, or accessibility labels without app-builder inventing those values.

`appBuilderInputReadiness` is the request-specific read model that projects selected ontology rows plus supplied inputs into satisfied, missing-required, missing-recommended, deferred, and rejected dependencies. Dependency rows include fine-grained input facets by default so an AI can ask for concrete missing facts; use `includeInputFacets: false` only for compact reads. Use readiness before asking an AI/user for more information or lowering source.

Input-readiness counts are dependency-level, not facet-level. A supplied direct field such as `labelText` may appear in `suppliedInputFacetIds` while the dependency row remains `missing-recommended` because another selected facet, such as `AccessibilityHelpError`, is still absent. Consumers should inspect dependency rows before treating aggregate counts as complete or absent input.

Facet-scoped supplied input only satisfies the dependency facets it names; contract-wide markers remain explicit for broad intakes. Concrete `facetPayloads` validate against modeled input-contract-detail schemas and surface payload validation rows/issues, so lowerers should not treat a coarse contract marker as proof of detailed domain, collection, policy, or accessibility payload.

`appBuilderInputContractDetail` is the payload/schema detail read model for input contracts and facets. Use it after readiness when an AI needs the modeled input shape; treat `to-be-determined`, `deferred`, and `not-caller-payload` states as honest boundaries rather than source-driving payload schemas.

`detail-helpers.ts` owns shared app-builder ontology detail projection row selection and id dedupe. New family detail projections should use `appBuilderSelectRows`, `appBuilderUniqueIds`, or `appBuilderUniqueRowsById` before adding local selector helpers.

`detail-joins.ts` owns reusable row-family joins for detail projections and should spend `relation-index.ts` for ontology graph traversal. New detail queries should use detail-joins for affordance/effect, follow-up affordance, application-pattern, collection, control, manifest, style, visual-policy, and control-realization joins instead of scanning row-family arrays locally.

SourcePlacement input facets should reuse source-plan substrate: SourceRoot names the target root/area, SourceTargetPath names one concrete preview/write artifact path, source naming exposes app/source name seeds plus SourcePatternParameterValue rows, source file layout names resource carrier/view-form policy rather than concrete paths, and source project tooling exposes SourcePlanProjectTooling shapes for dependencies, scripts, package manifests, tsconfig, and declarations.

CollectionIdentityPolicy input detail spends AppBuilderCollectionIdentityMode and AppBuilderCollectionIdentityUse. Do not turn ID binding into universal truth; require stable keys only when selected collection features need them.

CollectionQueryFeatures input detail spends AppBuilderCollectionFeatureId. Keep caller-selected collection features separate from AppBuilderCollectionFeatureRung, which is internal gradual-ascent ordering rather than public payload vocabulary.

`appBuilderAffordanceDetail` is the selected-move read model. Use it when an AI has chosen affordance rows and needs the factual join to readiness, payload detail, promised effects, associated application patterns, and declared follow-ups without source lowering or heuristic next-move ranking.

`appBuilderEffectContractDetail` is the selected promised-effect read model. Use it when an AI has selected effect rows and needs the inverse join back to promising affordances, those affordances' input readiness/payload detail, and nearby application patterns without executing verification or claiming satisfaction.

`APP_BUILDER_ONTOLOGY_ROW_DESCRIPTORS` is the shared row-ref/title/status descriptor list for readiness and target-catalog projections. Add new ontology families there instead of creating projection-local target lists that can omit policy, effect, input, or styling rows.

MCP app-builder tools should be thin forwarders over the semantic-runtime app-builder facade. Transport schemas may import public enum/value-list constants for validation, but they must not import catalog/lowering internals or invent MCP-local workflow policy.

Generic seed or intent defaults must not leak stale lowering choices into an explicitly selected composition. Once a composition is selected, its default Aurelia lowering is a baseline, while caller-supplied source-layout choices such as convention/decorator layer on top without changing composition identity.

Composition IDs should name reusable app-building mechanics, not emitted source carrier variants. Convention/decorator belongs to the Aurelia lowering selection and concrete SourcePattern key; a `*.convention` or `*.decorator` composition ID is a canary for recipe-key multiplication.

When resolving generated source from selected app-builder mechanics, compare requested Aurelia lowering through semantic mechanic axes. Source-layout axes such as app convention policy, resource carrier, view form, DOM encapsulation, and style policy should not disqualify an otherwise compatible composition; they are spent later by SourcePlan lowerers.

`custom-element-source-layout.ts` owns app-builder root/custom-element file-name, class-name, resource-name, Aurelia import, and decorator prelude decisions for convention/decorator carriers. New source-starting lowerers should spend that layout instead of copying `src/my-app.ts` or `src/app.ts` decisions locally.

`appBuilderCustomElementClassSource` owns the generated custom-element class wrapper for carrier-specific metadata. Empty generated classes should stay compact there instead of each source-lowering caller deciding whether to emit multiline empty class bodies.

The @aurelia/state collection path is a future explicit sibling of DI-state generated source. It uses plugin store ownership, store-state record modeling, store-scoped state binding, `.state`, `.dispatch`, and @aurelia/state project tooling; it should not be described as DI state or plain-domain composition.
Future domains should be added with paired seed-data strategy from the start: at minimum no-data plus one public small set, and optionally demo/inspection sets for fixture pressure.

Caller-domain source generation should prefer meaningful required slots over boilerplate slots. For current collection/form source lowerers, `entity-title` and `field-schema` are the required caller facts; entity type, collection member, and identity member are derived defaults that can still be overridden.

The old starter menu/golden lane was removed from public app-builder authority on 2026-06-03. Keep deleted starter menus, starter intents, seed profiles, domain presets, starter composition catalogs, starter builders, starter evidence bridges, and app-builder goldens absent unless the operator explicitly asks to rebuild them.

Current app-builder source work should route through ontology-catalog, target-catalog, input-readiness, input-contract-detail, source-lowering-preflight, source-lowering-invocation, source-lowering-composition, source-lowering-source-plan, part-menu, part-source-lowering-preview, and part-source-invocation.

Pressure fixtures that prove focused app-builder source-lowering/analyzer behavior live under packages/semantic-runtime/fixtures/pressure/app-builder-* and are generated by pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime fixtures:app-builder-pressure. Generated-app contract fixtures live under packages/semantic-runtime/fixtures/app-builder, are generated by pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime fixtures:app-builder-generated, and preserve exact compact public request inputs, compact public response snapshots, generated source, a detailed semantic manifest, expected effects, and semantic-runtime reopen/verification expectations. The verifier derives a detail-mode SourcePlan query from the stored compact request when it needs SourcePlan witnesses, control-use rows, expected-effect rows, or decision-bundle expansion rows; those flags should not be saved in `app-builder-request.json` unless the fixture is explicitly testing public detail mode. Start whole-set generated-app machine review from packages/semantic-runtime/fixtures/app-builder/generated-fixture-index.json; use packages/semantic-runtime/fixtures/app-builder/generated-fixture-index.md as the generated human-review companion with compact fixture counts and per-fixture links. The JSON index's compact `fixtureSummaryRows` include exact request/response/semantic manifest paths plus generated source/tooling paths, source-lowering targets, effect kinds, decision-bundle counts, decision-bundle input contract/facet rollups, policy-satisfaction candidate refs, separate `controlUseInventoryControlPatternIds` for ontology target coverage, and separate `controlUseInventoryLeafControlIds` for native-control evidence before the full fixture rows expand advertised request-field surfaces and actual source-lowering request-field usage rows with compact request paths/value shapes. The index also includes `sourceLoweringTargetRegistryCoverageRows`, `sourceLoweringRequestFieldRegistryCoverageRows`, `policySatisfactionCandidateCoverageRows`, `domainModelCoverageSummary`, `domainModelKindCoverageSummary`, and `domainModelCoverageRows`. Use the domain coverage rows to review entity/domain concentration, identity kind coverage, field value-kind coverage, relationship-kind coverage, action-kind coverage, action-scope coverage, value-set coverage, and seed-data coverage before adding another generated-app fixture variant. All `AppBuilderDomainRelationshipKind`, `AppBuilderDomainActionKind`, and action-scope values are now covered by generated app fixtures through explicit caller-owned command actions, router-backed route-navigation actions, relationship lookup/ownership/value display, and relationship-backed table columns; standalone navigation outside router-backed topology plus relationship editing/lifecycle/service workflows remain app-design/source-lowering frontier evidence, not fixture failures by themselves. Request-field coverage summaries partition unused fields into required, conditional-only, optional-only, and mixed conditional/optional counts so required gaps do not get blurred with refinement knobs. Start focused source-lowering pressure review from packages/semantic-runtime/fixtures/pressure/app-builder-source-lowering-fixture-index.json; it records the same shared request-field usage/registry coverage vocabulary for direct invocation/composition pressure calls. Its compact request-field summary is intentionally scoped to direct target-invocation and fragment-composition surfaces, and its detail rows should match that same scoped row set instead of carrying SourcePlan-preview wrapper fields. Those actual request-field usage rows are emitted through the shared source-lowering request-field helper, not materializer-owned maps. Generated-app fixture verification reruns stored requests, verifies the stored compact response snapshot, compares SourcePlan source/tooling text to tracked files, checks detailed contract row families through the derived detail query, and checks generated-source-quality canaries such as duplicate static template attributes and trailing whitespace before reopening the fixture through semantic-runtime, so same-input/source-output drift is caught outside app-builder lowerers. Persisted generated-fixture snapshots should deep-normalize the fixture root to `.` so review artifacts do not leak local workspace paths.

Generated fixture index rows now join explicit source-lowering targets and generated control-use coverage to compact recommendation-policy posture, applicability/evidence kind ids, and contextual executable policy-satisfaction candidate flags/state. Use `sourceLoweringTargetPolicyRows` for SourcePlan-declared targets and `generatedControlUsePolicyRows` for leaf/native controls that appear through generated `ControlUse` inventory, so generated output can be reviewed against recommendable versus contextual terrain without turning the fixture materializer into a hidden recommendation/defaulting selector. Policy rows produced from explicit SourcePlan target refs may report policy satisfaction as satisfied. Generated control-use policy rows may also satisfy contextual controls through exact target refs, explicit nested `fieldControlSelections`, or deterministic domain-field control input; pure control-use observation alone should still report missing selection. `fixtureSummaryRows` should expose policy-satisfaction count/source summaries by default, while detailed target refs and summaries remain in full fixture rows. The `minimal-app-shell-decorator` generated fixture is a narrow convention-policy/resource-carrier canary for explicit resource declarations, not permission to multiply every fixture across carrier variants. After recommendation-policy changes, regenerate `fixtures:app-builder-generated` so persisted fixture response snapshots do not retain stale policy projections.

`policy-satisfaction.ts` is the shared first-ring gate for contextual executable source-lowering targets. Exact target selection satisfies the current gate; broad/default preflight target sets may list contextual targets but should keep `canRequestSourceLowering=false` with `missing-explicit-selection` policy state. Source-lowering preflight should expose top-level policy gate required/missing/satisfied counts while keeping policy-satisfaction issues row-local, so MCP callers can skim broad/default answers without treating contextual gates as malformed request issues. Broad/default preflight rows with contextual policy gates should continue to `recommendation-policy` detail for those exact target refs, so an AI can inspect applicability/evidence before making an explicit selection. Recommendation-policy detail and source-lowering preflight must spend this shared predicate instead of redefining contextual executable terrain locally.

`sourceLoweringTargetRegistryCoverageRows` distinguish `source-lowering-target-ref` coverage from `generated-control-use` coverage. A target ref proves the public SourcePlan answer names an executable source-lowering target, including nested propagation such as field-group emitted form messages; generated control-use proves runtime/control-shaped rows that authored `control-use-inventory` can verify. Do not force static form-message paragraphs into control-use rows unless semantic-runtime gains a static-template message inventory schema.

`sourceLoweringRequestFieldRegistryCoverageRows` compare request-field usage to the app-builder request-field registry, including target-owned fields and SourcePlan-selection-owned fields such as component-pair envelopes when those rows are in the selected review scope. Treat unused optional/conditional fields as review pressure and used fields without registry ownership as a canary; do not turn the row family into a failing requirement that every optional request-field enum member has generated fixture coverage immediately. Coverage summaries carry `summarizedRegistryOwnerKinds` and `summarizedSurfaceKinds`; detail rows in a focused review index should match that same scoped row set, so read the scope before interpreting compact unused counts across generated-app versus focused-pressure fixture lanes. `policySatisfactionCandidateCoverageRows` show whether contextual executable policy-satisfaction candidates are covered by explicit source-lowering target refs, generated control-use rows, or both. Generated-app fixtures and focused source-lowering pressure fixtures should be read together before deciding whether a gap needs an end-user-shaped generated fixture, a focused pressure fixture, or only a deferred optional-row note.

`check:fixture-typecheck` now recognizes source-only semantic fixtures with `semantic-fixture.json` and `src/**/*.ts`. Use that lane for standalone SourcePlan artifacts such as `di-state-task-state` instead of adding fake generated package tooling just to make fixture typechecking discover the file.

Router-backed list/detail generated state lookup source should spend `DomainEntities.identityValueKind`. Route params stay string-valued at the Aurelia router boundary: numeric identities compare `String(item.id)` to the route param, while string identities compare the member directly without generic `String(...)` projection.

Router-backed generated fixtures are also a router-instruction materialization canary. If generated `load="route: ...; params.bind: ..."` links produce an open seam, lose route-node recognition, or report the route id as a route parameter value, fix the router LoadCustomAttribute route+params branch rather than making app-builder avoid inline load syntax.

The flat app-builder barrel should expose the current ontology, part/source substrate, and explicit source-lowering helpers only. Deleted starter/golden files should stay absent until the app-builder algebra is rebuilt from the ontology.
Source-lowering availability uses SourceLoweringImplemented, NotImplemented, and UnknownTarget. Treat sourceLoweringImplemented as the executable support bit; there is no starter-evidence bridge.

anchors.summary: Current leading app-builder v1 scope and prioritization packet; read before broad app-builder v1 implementation work.
Investigation answer that grounds immediate v1 substrate gaps behind the recalibration packet.
Control primitive completeness answer for native/display/control-affordance gaps exposed by the v1 canaries.
Read-only app-builder ontology catalog exposed as ontology-catalog without lowering source.
Typed domain/status/reason-authority filters for the read-only ontology-catalog query.
Shared normalizer for exact targetRefs plus AI-facing compact targetSelectors before input-readiness, target-catalog, or source-lowering preflight graph operations.
Full unfiltered app-builder ontology relation graph used by internal cross-domain projections such as input-readiness and effect targeting.
Full-graph relation lookup for rows leaving a selected ontology row.
Full-graph relation lookup for rows entering a selected ontology row.
Shared relation-graph bridge from selected ontology target refs to associated effect contract ids for catalog, preflight, invocation, and composition projections.
Operator-reviewable recommendation ranking for app-builder ontology rows without selecting blank-slate defaults.
Central policy projection for local defaulting candidates; not blank-slate or starter defaulting.
Operator-reviewable local defaulting-candidate policy table with scope and rationale rows separated from recommendation posture.
Public read-only recommendation-policy projection with target/filter inputs, compact counts by default, full rows on request, and contextual executable policy-satisfaction candidates.
Contextual executable-row classifier for policy/defaulting review; not a hidden source-lowering or default-selection gate.
Request-local decision/defaulting carrier that expands caller, operator-default, or framework-default choices into supplied inputs.
Expansion helper used by readiness, preflight, and source-lowering surfaces before input gates run.
Target-scoped expansion helper that prevents request-local decisions for one ontology row from globally satisfying neighboring source-lowering targets.
Registry-backed projection from row-local declared status to public app-builder ontology status.

records.summary: The 2026-06-07 app-builder v1 recalibration packet is the current leading scope and priority authority for app-builder v1 implementation.
records.rationale: The latest calibration found that app-builder work can look productive while drifting into maintenance or broad rung climbing before immediate v1 truthfulness gaps are closed. Atlas should route app-builder v1 work toward the packet, use older interviews as texture, and prevent fixture-flywheel or starter/golden memory from reasserting stale shape.
guidance: Read `.temp/app-builder-v1-scope-recalibration-packet-2026-06-07.md` first for app-builder v1 work until a later calibration supersedes it.

Use `.temp/app-builder-v1-investigation-answer-2026-06-07.md` and `.temp/app-builder-control-primitive-completeness-answer-2026-06-07.md` as immediate supporting answers; use older June 1-3 interviews as background texture when ambiguity remains.
Treat Atlas as a navigation and staleness-check substrate for this topic, not as permission to let older app-builder routes outrank the recalibration packet.

Immediate v1 slices after the routed-detail fix are runnable app startup/root awareness, control affordance expansion, validation policy floor, central default labels/messages, LLM/seed/local data handoff, @aurelia/state posture, lean accessibility, and existing control library/plugin detection/reporting. Keep routed-detail `with.bind`/taste as a regression canary: generated detail output should use explicit item-qualified bindings and state helper calls with item arguments, not broad `with.bind` plus `$parent.state.*($this)`.

Do not enum-first patch controls. Inspect the ontology, source-lowering request fields, policy/defaulting, fixtures, MCP presentation, and Atlas implications before declaring a control affordance complete.
Do not keep broad composition ascent ahead of these immediate substrate gaps. Rung advancement is the goal, but only after the packet's low-level blockers are visible or handled.
Visible defers for this packet include query-string state, toast/notification, full edit buffers, full i18n/localization, and remote fetch/server integration.
After meaningful app-builder product slices, update Atlas memory, Work Router terms/canaries, and reconciliation surfaces so future agents do not rediscover stale assumptions.

records.summary: App-builder should prefer read-only ontology breadth before adding source generation.
records.rationale: The operator explicitly preferred working blind across the ontology for a while rather than relying on one proving slice. Source-producing slices can always be made to pass; the harder problem is a coherent model that spans controls, collections, style inputs, domain input, state policy, routing policy, status/recommendation policy, manifests, SourcePlan effects, and verification.
guidance: Do not implement another generation path as the next move merely because one slice is easy to prove.

Use `ontology-catalog` as the first public read-only app-builder ontology surface. Earlier zero-lowering guidance was a phase marker; now treat source-lowering-implemented rows as deliberate executable targets that must spend modeled input contracts and status rows.

Add or reshape read-only rows first: status matrix, reason authority, policy axes, affordance/move graph, input contracts, supplied input provenance, application design patterns, collection source/query/table-column, control pattern/use, component/control manifest, and style input placeholders.
Use `target-catalog` when a caller needs selectable ontology rows with status/readiness facts before choosing what to inspect or lower.
When source generation resumes, it should spend known input contracts and policy rows rather than inventing the ontology while writing files.
A source slice is pressure, not proof. Keep holistic consistency and public explainability as the stronger acceptance condition.

records.summary: App-builder should accept and carry visual/style inputs rather than authoring a built-in visual fallback now.
records.rationale: The styling interview exposed a category boundary: semantic-runtime/app-builder should structure tokens, class hooks, utility-class policy, external design-system references, and supplied CSS/classes, while the external AI or caller usually authors the actual visual design. A built-in fallback would require product taste decisions that are intentionally deferred until real MCP test drives produce operator-approved material.
guidance: Treat visual/style input as an exploratory contract frontier. Names and fields should emerge from standards, AI design tooling, Aurelia APIs, and experimentation rather than confident invention.
No visual input supplied should be represented as a normal missing or recommended input state, not as an error and not as permission to invent hidden CSS taste.

VisualClassHooks is the first modeled visual payload because source lowerers can spend it mechanically as supplied class tokens and data-* attributes. `component-pair-task-table-visual-hooks` proves that a target-scoped visual-style decision reaches the CollectionTable SourcePlan HTML without becoming global input, generated CSS, or a design-system fallback. `component-pair-task-draft-field-variety` proves the same payload family across NativeSubmitForm form, field-group, label, field-control, field-message, and submit-button targets, with explicit NativeSubmitForm plus delegated FieldGroup target refs. Keep visual tokens, CSS fragments, and design-system references visibly TBD until their policy and consumers are grounded.
Do not make Tailwind an unconditional dependency. Tailwind-friendly output can be an explicit option; neutral tokens and class hooks remain the safer interchange substrate.
Shadow DOM and CSS Modules remain real Aurelia mechanisms but should not be the default for generated local controls unless context or existing app facts call for them.

A future design/style-flow engine may join class/style bindings, tokens, CSS modules, Shadow DOM parts, and manifests, but that is a deferred frontier rather than a blocker for the app-builder ontology.

records.summary: Semantic-runtime should own a canonical component/control manifest and adapt outward to external formats.
records.rationale: Custom Elements Manifest, VS Code custom data, web-types, shadcn/v0 registries, Storybook, and Figma Code Connect are useful projections, but Aurelia-specific facts are central rather than extensions: bindables, binding modes, value channels, resource visibility, local dependencies, template-controller compatibility, SourcePlan provenance, expected effects, app-builder pattern contracts, and MCP/IDE loci.
guidance: Do not make Custom Elements Manifest the canonical semantic-runtime model. Use it as an adapter/projection when external interoperability needs it.
The canonical model should be able to describe generated local controls, inline ControlUse rows, existing app controls, and detectable external web components when metadata is available.

Authored native inline controls now have a public semantic-runtime app query, `control-use-inventory`, backed by runtime binding value-channel/data-flow products plus static submit-control joins through containing form submit listeners. Keep generated control-use rows and authored-source inventory convergent, but do not build a second app-builder template parser.

`control-use-inventory` participates in public app-query continuations: authored control rows can drill back to binding value-channel/data-flow evidence, and binding rows can drill forward to the control inventory they support; static submit-button rows should expose their containing-form action channel instead of pretending the button owns a direct listener.

`ResourceDefinitions` is the current app-world witness for `ComponentApiManifest` terrain. Public app-query continuations bridge ResourceDefinitions to app-builder `control-manifest-detail(ComponentApiManifest)` and `effect-contract-detail(ComponentManifestPublication)`; do not add a duplicate component-manifest app query unless resource-definition rows prove structurally insufficient.

`control-manifest-detail` exposes direct manifest-witness effect contracts, not every source-plan or reopen effect reachable through coordinating application patterns. Use broad `appBuilderEffectContractIdsForTargetRef(...)` for target/source-preflight effect association and direct manifest-witness helpers for selected manifest detail.

MCP pass-through for `control-manifest-detail` is a transport proof, not a second manifest model: the MCP schema accepts the detail envelope and the adapter forwards it to semantic-runtime so the same direct manifest-witness rows reach public tools.
Keep generated and analyzed source convergent: inline native controls and wrapper components should be describable through the same semantic control contract when possible.
A first manifest skeleton can stay read-only and status-rich. It does not need to generate source or fully model CSS/style-flow.

ControlRealizationPolicy is now a targetable read-only Control-domain row family. ControlUse, ControlAccessibilityContract, ControlValueContract, ControlStyleContract, and ComponentManifest remain manifest/control terrain that can deepen without becoming source lowerers by default.

ControlManifestFieldDescriptor rows are selected-manifest contract vocabulary. They name fields to expose or prove later without publishing external manifests, component APIs, control-use products, or style hooks by themselves.

records.summary: First-ring app-builder controls should be native-first and Aurelia value-channel grounded, but current value-kind coverage is not the full control/display affordance universe.
records.rationale: The control-design interview, WAI-ARIA research, and June 7 control-completeness calibration support native web controls plus Aurelia value/checked/select/class/style channels as the first-ring substrate. Rich APG widgets remain visible but deferred until their behavior and accessibility contracts are modeled; email/url/tel/password/search and display-link affordances should be treated as explicit v1 control pressure instead of hidden text variants.
guidance: Start v1 control ontology with native form controls, list/card/table, loading/empty/error structures, and mature platform APIs when clearly useful. Per the June 7 recalibration, do not assume current enum coverage is complete: email, url, tel, password, search, and display-link affordances should pressure the ontology, policy/defaulting, lowerers, fixtures, and MCP detail answers as explicit cases.
Reference Aurelia value, checked, select, writeback, class/style, proxy observation, getter observation, and template-controller capabilities instead of inventing a parallel form abstraction.

Scalar native control patterns, native choice controls, native button event controls, form-message fragments, inline field-group composition, and the adjacent DomainCommandAction class-member target currently have source-lowering-implemented source invocation. Scalar controls lower through `appBuilderSourceLoweringInvocation` after domain-field input is supplied; choice controls additionally need field-local options, a named compatible domain value set, or explicit valueDomainExpression; native buttons need a domain action plus button text/accessibility label, emit an explicit native buttonType that defaults event-handler buttons to type=button, and lower through the event-listener part callback; form messages need accessibility help/error/status payloads or explicit message text/kind and lower through the authored template composer; field groups wrap a derived or explicitly selected native field control with label and help/error/status relationships, using fieldset/legend for radio-group and checkbox-list inner controls. Field value-kind policy maps to leaf controls through `appBuilderDomainFieldControlId`, and leaf controls map back to control patterns through `appBuilderControlPatternIdForLeafControlId`; lowerers and fixture tooling should spend those shared helpers instead of recreating local value-kind-to-control-pattern switches. The same field-kind fallback set must stay reflected in recommendation/defaulting policy: text, number, date, boolean, single-select, multi-select, and textarea are ordinary defaults after field kind and option data are supplied; range, radio-group, and checkbox-list remain contextual/explicit alternatives. `AccessibilityHelpError.fieldName` scopes help/error/status payloads to one generated field group; unscoped payloads remain target-global for standalone/simple cases. Native Submit Form composition rejects stale field-scoped `ControlAccessibility` and `VisualClassHooks` payloads when their `fieldName` is not selected, and rejects submit-button visual hooks scoped to a different action, so caller inputs are either spent by generated source or surfaced as issues. Direct standalone grouped controls use fieldset/legend too; embedded grouped fieldsets spend FieldGroup visual hooks, while FieldControl visual hooks are applied to descendant repeated input elements. Field-group static fieldControlId derivation is a known reusable-component canary: do not solve it by weakening accessibility relationships or by inventing hidden runtime id machinery; design an explicit policy when wrapper/control-library generation needs it. DomainCommandAction lowers explicit caller-owned `methodParameters` plus conditional `methodBodyStatements` to a TypeScript class-member fragment; derived behavior is limited to first-ring local numeric-id create actions and first-ring entity-complete single-boolean row mutations for local collection state. It does not emit control-use rows. Standalone scalar and select controls spend direct labelText, supplied accessibility-label input, or selected field title as aria-label; grouped radio/checkbox-list controls use that same label text as legend text. Embedded field-group inner controls must not also emit aria-label because the group owns the visible label plus help/error/status relationships. Leaf controls should narrow input readiness to AccessibilityLabels, while field-group keeps AccessibilityLabels plus AccessibilityHelpError. Direct delegated control fragments preserve part-source origin, while composed button/message/field-group fragments carry app-builder source-lowering origin for SourcePlan contribution inspection. Direct accessibility request fields such as buttonText/labelText/messageText are preflight-normalized into explicit caller input facets rather than forming a second readiness lane. Source-lowering-implemented control targets that can attach field-control, button, or message visual hooks should expose VisualStyleInput/VisualClassHooks as recommended readiness input because the lowerer can spend those hooks mechanically; this is not permission to invent CSS. Containing-form submit workflow is executable through Native Submit Form and public control-use `containing-form-submit` rows; standalone submit variants outside that composition, wrapper controls, and state-dispatch actions still need explicit policy before becoming executable.

Source-lowering invocation now has explicit admission and routing frames. Future invocation work should reuse `sourceLoweringInvocationPreflightFrame`, `sourceLoweringInvocationTargetFrame`, and `lowerSourceLoweringInvocationTarget` instead of adding target/preflight gates directly to leaf-control, button, message, field-group, or application-pattern lowerers.

Generated control-use rows should preserve source-lowering provenance for action/value channels. When a composition emits a button or submit control directly, carry `handlerExpressionSource` with the same explicit-request versus selected-action-name vocabulary used by source-lowering invocation instead of letting generated fixture manifests collapse handler provenance to null.

Leaf control and field-group lowerers now expose explicit selection/render phase frames. Future source-lowering work should reuse `fieldControlSourceSelectionFrame`, `fieldControlRenderedFrame`, `fieldGroupSelectionFrame`, and `fieldGroupRenderedFragments` concepts rather than reintroducing local value-domain, numeric-constraint, accessibility, visual-hook, or message-wrapper decisions in composed form lowerers.

Leaf-control descriptor rows own generated field-label container and visual-hook descendant placement through `fieldLabelContainerKind` and `visualHookDescendantTagName`. Source-lowering invocation should read those catalog facts instead of recreating local checkbox/radio switches; control-manifest detail exposes the same fields for Atlas/MCP inspection.

Generated choice controls should default to Aurelia `model.bind` option identity because it preserves non-string values and object identity. Native `value.bind` option semantics are legal caller-selected override pressure for scalar string-like choices, but app-builder should not make them the default or use them to fake object-valued matcher coverage.

Numeric field constraints are caller/domain facts, not visual policy or invented defaults. `numericConstraints.minimum`, `numericConstraints.maximum`, and `numericConstraints.step` lower through typed part slots as native `min`, `max`, and `step` attributes; range preflight reports missing bounds as target-specific requirements and range source invocation requires all three so sliders do not silently inherit browser defaults, while plain number inputs spend only the constraints supplied.

Native Submit Form now has source-lowering-implemented source-lowering composition. It spends field-group source invocation for selected fields plus event-listener source invocation for form submit, and its readiness should expose VisualStyleInput/VisualClassHooks because the composition lowerer can pass form, field, button, and message hooks into member fragments. When caller visual inputs are target-scoped, generated fixtures explicitly target both `application-pattern:native-submit-form` and the delegated `control-pattern:field-group`; field-group invocation also propagates nested `control-pattern:form-message` target refs when it emits help/error/status fragments. Static form-message paragraphs are generated source target coverage, not authored `control-use-inventory` rows; static-template message inventory would need a separate analyzer schema instead of fixture-local row fiction. Generated Native Submit Form fixtures may use ordinary actionless `submit.trigger` form source because Aurelia app-root prevents native navigation for actionless forms by default, with `allowActionlessForm` as the app-global override; do not mask that framework capability by adding fixture-local `$event.preventDefault()` handlers. It is still a composition surface over native controls, not permission to generate wrapper/rich controls, invented CSS, or full submit workflow lifecycles by default.

Loading / Empty / Error State now has source-lowering-implemented source-lowering composition. It spends explicit promise/status request fields through promise, pending, then, catch, and conditional structural-part lowerers, emits one direct-branch `promise.bind` region, and keeps empty-state `if.bind` inside the fulfilled branch. Request text and branch-local selection flow through `loadingEmptyErrorSelectedInputs(...)`, then missing required fields and unsafe locals are rejected by `loadingEmptyErrorSelectionIssues(...)` before structural lowering. It should expose VisualStyleInput/VisualClassHooks as recommended readiness input for status-region, pending, empty, and error hook targets, but it must not claim a domain-model dependency or invent async data sources, empty semantics, dynamic error rendering, or lifecycle timing.

`AsyncDataSource` is the adjacent explicit source-lowering invocation for generated promise-member state. Use it when caller input supplies the member name, promise type, and initializer expression so `promise.bind` regions bind a stable promise-valued member instead of a template method call. It does not invent fetching, service boundaries, retry, refresh, or lifecycle behavior; those stay with later application-design patterns or caller-owned code.

Use the public `control-use-inventory` app query when an existing app or fixture needs authored native control inventory. It reuses runtime binding value-channel/data-flow products and static submit-control joins and should remain convergent with generated `AppBuilderControlUseInventoryRow` rows without becoming a separate parser or source-generation path.

`control-use-inventory` is part of the app-query continuation graph and semantic contract suite; future wrappers/rich widgets should join that inquiry path instead of adding isolated app-builder-only row tables.

When app-builder public rows need the full runtime binding value-channel id set, consume the observation-owned `RUNTIME_BINDING_VALUE_CHANNEL_KINDS` list instead of enumerating const enum members locally.
Rich widgets such as combobox, menu, slider, treegrid, tooltip, and complex dialogs should be modeled as later-ring APG contract work, not generated by default.

Inline versus wrapper is a realization policy, not a different semantic control concept. Use ControlRealizationPolicy rows and control-pattern-detail filters for that axis instead of splitting the control value contract.
Inline controls may eventually use marker/data attributes, but semantic-runtime should still understand ordinary HTML/Aurelia bindings when markers are absent.

records.summary: Classify app-builder interview answers by effect horizon before turning them into work.
records.rationale: The app-builder interviews intentionally mix small API invariants, near-term canaries, structural fixture lanes, roadmap frontiers, and deferred v1 polish. Treating all answers as one flat task list causes either over-scoping or premature down-scoping. Atlas should route the decision effect first, then choose the right work surface.
guidance: Use `immediate-invariant` for decisions that should be encoded now in public contracts, schemas, docs, or API behavior. Example: decision-bundle counts are visible by default while expansion rows are opt-in detail output.

Use `near-canary` for decisions that should be tested soon through the smallest honest substrate. Current app-builder canaries are source-placement, convention/resource policy, and native form-control leafs rather than whole starter-app profiles.

Use `structural-lane` for handles that should exist when implementation starts wanting a home but should not become a subsystem too early. Current example: `packages/semantic-runtime/fixtures/app-builder` as the tracked generated-app contract lane with exact inputs, generated source, manifests, expected effects, and reopen verification.

Use `roadmap-frontier` for multi-hour or multi-day design work that must stay visible but should not be smuggled into a small slice. Current examples include policy satisfaction, generated fixture contracts, findings/suppression policy, design/style integration, and app-builder defaulting/profile design.
Use `deferred-polish` for v1/MCP ergonomics work that should not drive the current substrate. Current examples include preflight rejection example payloads and naive-AI presentation defaults.

Before acting on an interview answer, ask which effect class it has, which surface owns it, whether it is already encoded, and what trigger should activate it. Do not turn roadmap frontiers into hidden behavior during an immediate invariant pass.

When a future run finds a decision in `.temp`, promote the durable essence into Atlas memory and Work Router with an effect class instead of leaving it as scratch prose or flattening it into the next code task.

If an interview decision turns into a multi-hour substrate gap, create or update a named pressure-frontier with anchors and nextActionPolicy instead of hiding the gap inside a small source-lowering or fixture patch.

If an interview decision turns into a fixture requirement, decide whether it belongs in focused pressure fixtures or `fixtures/app-builder` generated-app contracts. Generated-app contracts require exact public inputs, generated source, manifest/effects, and reopen verification.

records.rationale: The first generated app-builder routed fixture typecheck caught string seed IDs flowing into a generated numeric identity constructor. guidance: Keep route params string-valued at the router boundary and stringify identity values for lookup until a richer identity policy proves necessary.
Do not infer identity type from one seed record. Any source lowerer that emits typed entity identities, seed-backed state, or identity lookup code should require explicit caller/domain identity kind.

Target-specific source-lowering preflight requirements should run only after coarse input readiness is ready; broad/default source-lowering discovery should remain a menu instead of becoming partial because caller inputs are absent.

When adding new identity-consuming source lowerers, thread the identity value kind through domain descriptors, materialization, input-contract detail, preflight, seed-record validation, generated source, generated fixtures, and contracts in one slice.
