records.summary: Component-pair SourcePlan output can now be elevated to a runnable app shell through an explicit public appShell envelope.
records.rationale: The generated fixture lane needs reviewable end-user-shaped app output. `sourceLoweringComponentPair` still supports component/app-section previews, and its optional `appShell` envelope now mounts the generated pair as the Aurelia root component with baseline project tooling, SourcePlan witnesses, and reopen expected effects. This keeps runnable fixtures on a first-class public source-plan path instead of fixture-local glue.
guidance: Use `sourceLoweringComponentPair.appShell` when a generated component pair should become a runnable app contract; leave it absent for component/app-section previews.
Keep app identity explicit through `SourceNaming.appName`; app-builder should reject missing app names for runnable app shells rather than inventing them.

Keep component-pair TypeScript placement explicit through `SourceTargetPath` and HTML placement explicit through `templatePath`; generated fixtures should store both the exact request and the public response snapshot.
Use `appBuilderRootCustomElementPairSourcePlan` and `appBuilderComponentPairAppShellExpectedEffects` rather than rebuilding entrypoint/tooling/reopen behavior locally.

Use `component-pair-task-draft-app-shell` as the current generated-app canary for scalar local view-model draft state plus a native submit-form save command assembled through component-pair source lowering; standalone form fixtures must not request local collection state unless a visible template projection consumes that collection. Use `component-pair-task-draft-field-variety` as the native field-control variety canary for all first-ring native field controls: default text/number/date/boolean/select controls plus explicit `fieldControlSelections` for textarea, range, radio-group, and checkbox-list; it also proves explicit domain-field defaults, numeric constraints, finite option type aliases, field-scoped `ControlAccessibility` help/status messages, grouped choice `fieldset`/`legend` source shape, native radio grouping through a shared static `name`, and caller-supplied VisualClassHooks for form, field-group, label, field-control, field-message, and submit-button targets without adding hidden collection mutation. Use `component-pair-task-draft-explicit-field-overrides` as the nested field-control caller override canary: NativeSubmitForm `fieldControlSelections` carry caller-supplied ids, labels, messages, value-domain expressions, option locals, option value/label expressions, and option binding policy into delegated FieldGroup source lowering without forcing a manual one-control invocation or hidden seed data. Use `component-pair-task-preferences-named-value-sets` as the reusable option-domain canary: composed choice controls select `valueSetName` by explicit caller input, use the default `model.bind` option identity path, and component-pair assembly applies those selections to local view-model state only when the selected field has no field-local option source. Keep always-visible error/alert messages in focused pressure fixtures until validation/display-state policy is modeled for generated apps. Use `component-pair-task-list-local-collection` and `component-pair-task-card-local-collection` as the list/card canaries for explicit caller-supplied local collection state plus `CollectionDisplayFields`; these prove that list/card/table are distinct collection projections rather than table aliases and that read-only boolean display checkbox control-use rows are not table-only. Use `component-pair-contact-card-string-id` as the non-task/string-identity collection-card canary so generated-app review does not silently overfit to task fields, numeric identity, or task-domain naming. Use `component-pair-task-table-local-collection` as the table canary for explicit caller-supplied local collection state plus `CollectionTableColumns` and read-only boolean display checkbox control-use rows. Use `component-pair-task-table-local-sort` as the first local collection query-rung canary: table metadata marks a field sortable, `sortHandlerExpressions` supplies the header handler, and a `DomainCommandAction` invocation supplies the collection-scoped method body; this proves explicit handler wiring, not invented query-state generation. Use `component-pair-task-table-local-filter` as the first local filter/search query-rung canary: table metadata marks a field filterable, `filterBindingExpressions` supplies the header search binding, local view-model state emits a filter member plus filtered-collection getter, and semantic-runtime reopen sees the generated native search input as a text-value control-use row. Use `component-pair-task-table-local-pagination` as the first local pagination query-rung canary: `CollectionQueryFeatures.pageSize` and explicit pagination control request fields generate local page state, a paged collection getter, table-foot pagination controls, native previous/next button control-use rows, typecheck, and reopen expected effects without inventing page size or method names. Use `component-pair-task-table-row-selection` as the first local scalar row-selection canary: explicit `CollectionIdentityPolicy` plus table checked/toggle/label request fields generate selected-id state and reopen as a checkbox control-use row with a direct change handler. Use `component-pair-task-table-batch-actions` as the first local batch-action canary: explicit `RowSelection`, scalar `CollectionIdentityPolicy`, and caller-supplied `batchActionControls` render a table-owned toolbar without claiming select-all, cross-page, service-backed, or richer workflow semantics. Use `component-pair-task-table-row-action` as the entity-scoped table action canary: `CollectionTableColumns.actionName` plus `actionHandlerExpressions` lower to `complete(taskItem)`, and a one-row `DomainCommandAction.methodParameters` entry lets the narrow entity-complete source-lowering path derive the single boolean mutation without generated apps carrying test-shaped parameters or raw method body text. Use `component-pair-task-command-actions` as the broad explicit command canary: update/delete/archive/assign row buttons and submit/refresh top-level buttons spend caller-owned `DomainActions`, explicit `DomainCommandAction.methodBodyStatements`, and local collection entity mutability from mutating action `inputFieldNames`; this proves action-kind transport and source wiring, not service/router integration or app-builder-invented behavior. Use `component-pair-task-table-visual-hooks` as the collection visual input and target-scoped decision-bundle canary: `VisualClassHooks` supplies structural class/data hooks scoped to the CollectionTable target and generated HTML spends them without generated CSS. Use `component-pair-task-table-loading-empty-error` as the async region canary: loading/empty/error lowering spends explicit promise/status request fields and an explicit nested fulfilled-content collection-table composition without inventing async data or lifecycle behavior. Use `component-pair-task-section-create-and-table` as the visible create-to-collection child-composition boundary canary: AppSection spends explicit `childCompositions` to combine a native submit form and collection table in one section, while child form/table lowerers still receive their target-scoped supplied inputs. Use `component-pair-task-section-create-and-async-table` as the nested visible create-to-async-collection canary: AppSection combines a native submit form with a loading/empty/error region whose fulfilled branch owns a collection table through `fulfilledContentComposition`, proving request capture, contribution provenance, and manifest verification without turning the section into a named starter. Generated app-builder fixture verification checks request idempotency for tracked source/tooling files and generated contract row families such as `sourcePlanWitnessRows`, and it also checks duplicate static template attributes and trailing whitespace as generated-source-quality canaries outside app-builder lowerers. Collection, routed-list, and detail display lowerers should spend domain field value kind and empty-state branch semantics deliberately: boolean fields render as disabled native checkboxes with `checked.to-view` rather than raw `true`/`false` interpolation, and any generated native display control should also surface a matching control-use/value-channel row with truthful provenance such as `selected-collection-field`. Routed list/detail display labels should not encode layout whitespace in semantic label text. Routed list route metadata titles should spend the same shared source-name collection title vocabulary as generated navigation/heading text. Routed list routes should use sibling `else` collection content when they emit an empty state unless a future explicit display-value policy says otherwise.

Use `component-pair-task-draft-object` as the rooted local draft-object canary: `bindingRootExpression` spends caller-owned receiver vocabulary, component-pair local state emits a typed draft object member, and native form controls bind through `taskDraft.title` / `taskDraft.done` while verification sees those rooted value channels. This is not full edit-buffer, dirty-state, undo/redo, validation, or service-save support.

Use `component-pair-task-assignment-section` as the first local relationship canary: AppSection combines a create form and table for a primary entity, spends `DomainRelationships.reference-one` to materialize the related entity collection, renders the related entity through a select control, emits lookup/label helpers, and spends a relationship-backed table column instead of treating relationships as field binding overrides.

Use `component-pair-task-reviewers-section` as the first local `reference-many` relationship canary: AppSection combines a create form and table for a primary entity, spends a choice-set local field that stores related string identity values, renders a native multi-select over the related collection, emits related-array and joined-label helpers, spends a relationship-backed table column, and keeps ownership/nested lifecycle semantics deferred instead of pretending many-valued references are owned child records.

Use `component-pair-task-schedule-section` as the first local `owns-one` relationship canary: AppSection renders a primary entity table, spends a required nested child seed object as a typed owned object on each parent entity, renders a relationship-backed table label column, and still keeps owned child editing and lifecycle/state-machine semantics deferred.

Use `component-pair-task-checkpoints-section` as the first local `owns-many` relationship canary: AppSection renders a primary entity table, spends nested child seed records as typed owned arrays on each parent entity, renders a relationship-backed table label column, and still keeps owned child editing and lifecycle/state-machine semantics deferred.

Use `component-pair-task-effort-section` as the first local `nested-value-object` relationship canary: AppSection renders a primary entity table, spends nested caller seed objects as typed identityless values on each parent entity, renders a relationship-backed table label column, and still keeps value-object editing and lifecycle/state-machine semantics deferred.

Collection-backed local submit/create forms derive finite option aliases from the collection entity type because the emitted entity source owns those aliases. Local-only scalar submit forms may still derive finite option aliases from the component class, and explicit `optionTypeName` or reusable `valueSetName` input remains caller-owned vocabulary.

The async generated fixtures now pair Loading / Empty / Error State composition with explicit `AsyncDataSource` invocation. Generated `promise.bind` regions should bind stable promise-valued members such as `taskItemsPromise`, while the promise member initializer remains caller input. Do not regress these canaries back to template method calls or let the loading-state lowerer invent async data.

Use `component-pair-task-table-service-loading` as the narrow explicit service collection canary: component-pair source lowering can emit a support service file, import it, resolve it through DI, and initialize an explicit async data member that feeds loading/empty/error table composition. Use `component-pair-task-table-service-refresh` as the adjacent refresh canary: it combines explicit service collection support, mutable `AsyncDataSource` promise state, target-scoped action feedback, a native refresh button, local feedback state, and `DomainCommandAction` structured service-call fields without raw method body text. Use `component-pair-task-table-service-filter` as the first service-backed query-refinement canary: the service collection request spends explicit `collectionEntityName`, `filterMethods`, method/field/parameter descriptors, and a command call with `serviceCallArgumentExpressions` to update the same promise-valued data member. Use `component-pair-task-section-service-create` as the first service-backed write canary: the service collection request spends explicit `createMethods`, generated service source owns numeric identity assignment and collection mutation, and the component command calls the service through structured service-call fields to replace the mutable promise-valued table member. Use `component-pair-task-table-service-complete` as the first service-backed row-update canary: the service collection request spends explicit `updateMethods`, keeps public records readonly over private mutable backing records, and carries row-local table context into a structured service call that refreshes the mutable promise-valued table member. Use `component-pair-task-section-service-create-and-complete` as the first combined service section canary: one generated service collection spends explicit create/update methods while a native submit form, row-level action feedback, and promise-backed table share the same mutable async collection member. Use `component-pair-task-section-service-filter-create-complete` as the ordered mixed query-preserving section canary: AppSection `childContent` interleaves a native submit form, direct all/open/completed query button invocations, action feedback statuses, and a promise-backed table while the service spends explicit filter/create/update methods, component-pair service support emits a query-state member plus reload method, and create/update commands refresh through that active query context. Use `component-pair-task-section-service-search` as the first service-backed text query canary: the service collection spends an explicit `text-contains` filter predicate, component-pair service support emits string query state plus a reload method, a field-group text input binds that query state, and search/clear command buttons refresh the promise-backed table through structured query-state actions. Use `component-pair-task-section-service-search-create-complete` as the combined text-query/write canary: create and row completion commands refresh through the active search reload method instead of resetting the promise-backed table to the unfiltered service result. These prove support-file assembly, service class/source modeling, service topology expected effects, narrow explicit reload/filter/create/update/text-search wiring, active query-state preservation across basic query controls and simple service writes, mixed composition/invocation pressure, reload-only query commands, and caller-owned service call arguments/status feedback; they do not claim remote fetch integration, retries, caching, broad sort/pagination/totals service-backed query UI, validation/toast policy, optimistic UI, full CRUD, edit-buffer/dirty-state policy, or lifecycle-state-machine behavior.

`component-pair-task-table-loading-empty-error` also proves nested composition input inheritance: collection-table scoped `VisualClassHooks` declared at the component-pair decision bundle level survive the loading/empty/error wrapper and are spent by the fulfilled-content table. Keep this canary when changing `sourceLoweringCompositionChildSuppliedInputs` or fulfilled-content lowering.

Component-pair local draft state is demand-driven by actual selected fields. Direct `LocalViewModelState` SourcePlan previews still emit all supplied fields, but component-pair assembly should derive local draft fields from composition-selected fields, direct field invocations, and declared action `inputFieldNames`; if a selected action omits `inputFieldNames`, keep all fields conservatively so explicit method bodies cannot reference missing state. Use `component-pair-task-draft-explicit-field-overrides` as the compact subset canary.

Generated TypeScript class-member fragments should format simple one-line field runs compactly while keeping arrays, methods, decorators, and multiline fragments separated. `sourceLoweringClassMemberInvocations` remains caller/source-order authority; fixture requests should order state-like async members before command methods when that is the cleaner generated source shape.

Generated domain entity classes should stay raw-domain by default. Presentation helpers such as date/choice display accessors are source-shape consequences owned by the surface that actually reads them; routed browse/detail may opt in, while create-only local collection state and DI-state class source should not emit unused label getters or title maps.

Generated domain entity construction should stay compact for tiny records and named for wide records. `AppBuilderDomainEntityConstructionInputStyle` owns that source-shape boundary: small entities may use positional constructor parameters, but wider entities should use `EntityInit` object input and multiline named seed rows so generated app code does not teach brittle argument-order maintenance. Generated domain entity fields should be readonly by default; component-pair local collection source may mark only fields mutated by actually emitted entity-scoped command invocations as public. The seed-data side is centralized in `appBuilderDomainCollectionInitializerSource`; the narrow first-ring local create command reuses the same construction helper, and the narrow first-ring entity-complete command may derive one typed row boolean mutation. Generated fixture seed rows should read like ordinary app-domain records, not descriptions of the fixture, visual hooks, row-action behavior, async loading, or verification pressure. Sort, service actions, save/update flows, status transitions, multi-field commands, and other behavior remain caller-owned until richer command lowerers exist.

Use `di-state-task-state` as the direct DI-state-class generated fixture canary. It covers the executable `application-pattern:di-state-class` SourcePlan target through `source-lowering-target-ref` registry coverage while intentionally emitting only a standalone state TypeScript file from caller domain/seed/placement input, not app-shell/router wiring.

Use the focused `app-builder-source-lowering-gallery` pressure fixture to cover explicit source-lowering request fields that should not become generated-app defaults. Read it together with the generated-app fixture index before treating request-field coverage as complete; generated fixtures now cover end-user-shaped service create and row-update fields while focused pressure covers low-level override fields. Treat combined request-field coverage as lowerer contract coverage, not recommendation breadth.

Keep the focused source-lowering gallery sectioned by coverage purpose. `fragmentGalleryFragmentSections` groups local state, class-member lowerings, and template lowering sections; control, collection, status, form, and app-section lowerings should stay named as fixture-pressure sections rather than becoming public app-builder choices or another starter/profile surface.

`AppSection` is an explicit composition boundary, not a named starter/profile/default. It wraps caller-selected child compositions, preserves child composition results and control-use rows, and should not grow app-shell wiring, routing, generated CSS, lifecycle behavior, or hidden neighbor selection policy.

`sourceLoweringComponentPair.sourceLoweringTemplateInvocations` is the direct template-invocation lane for component pairs. It exists for small target-specific affordances next to a composition, such as a reset button next to a form-save draft composition; use `component-pair-task-draft-reset-button` as the canary and keep richer object matcher/edit-buffer gaps recorded rather than faked through scalar value sets.

Generated fixture indexes include `sourceLoweringRequestFieldReviewSummary` and `sourceLoweringRequestFieldReviewRows` for unused generated-app request fields. Treat those rows as fixture-review posture: they can say a field is pressure-covered, covered by rooted draft-object generation, deferred for full edit-buffer modeling, or deferred for object-valued choice/domain relationship modeling without turning optional request-field counts into mechanical generated-fixture obligations; a nonzero needs-review count is the canary to classify new unused fields.

Component-pair SourcePlan lowering is intentionally split into phase frames: direct selection, nested source lowering, local-state derivation, fragment assembly, issue aggregation, SourcePlan artifact assembly, and public aggregate projection. Future component-pair fixes should land in the phase that owns the semantic question instead of growing `lowerComponentPairSourcePlan` back into an opaque transaction.

records.summary: Use the app-builder status matrix and gradual-ascent rule before adding options or lowerers.
records.rationale: The interviews repeatedly found that optional, deferred, default, source-lowering support, source-lowering, and recommendable were being conflated. The status matrix is the guardrail that lets the ontology map the terrain early while implementation climbs deliberately.
guidance: Before adding an option, decide whether it is modeled, sourceLoweringImplemented, recommendable/contextual/deferred/avoid/analysis-only, what applicability/context rows make it truthful, what evidence rows back it, whether it requires explicit input, and whether policy should admit it as a defaultingCandidate. Request-local decision bundles are the current defaulting carrier; blank-slate/new-app named profiles remain deferred. Keep decision-bundle counts visible by default, but make expansion rows opt-in detail output.
Do not use optional to mean source lowering is absent. Optional means available but not selected by default.
If a source-lowering surface has required request fields, the target status must report requiresExplicitInput=true; catalog-integrity treats the opposite as status-matrix drift.
Modeled rows without source lowering may be visible publicly as provisional or TBD. Do not hide uncertainty from the AI-facing surface.

Deferred and source-backed reasons need concrete evidence. Use operator, source-lowering, source-plan substrate, deterministic existing-app analysis, framework capability, control-manifest contract, web-platform, corpus/research, or TBD evidence rows; the legacy source-backed evidence lane should stay at zero unless a new unresolved source-backed row is intentionally left review-visible.
Application design patterns, collection features, visual policy, plugin choices, and control realization choices should all spend this status vocabulary.

Use `pnpm --filter @aurelia-ls/semantic-runtime policy:app-builder-review` as the cheap count/projection refresh after touching ontology rows, recommendation policy, source-lowering registry rows, or defaulting-candidate policy. The generated review includes focused source-lowering request-field rows, contextual source-lowering policy-satisfaction candidate rows, and defaulting-candidate policy rows with scope/rationale so policy/defaulting work can see concrete executable contextual targets and local fallback candidates without merging request fields into durable input facets or hidden defaults.
